title: Português de Portugal rumo ao DIPLE

days.title: Pretérito perfeito I
days.objective: Learn the regular pretérito perfeito simples of -AR, -ER and -IR verbs and use it to say what happened yesterday.
days.paretoFocus: Three regular ending sets (-ei/-aste/-ou/-ámos/-aram, -i/-este/-eu/-emos/-eram, -i/-iste/-iu/-imos/-iram) unlock the past tense for most Portuguese verbs.
drills: Conjugate 'falar', 'comer' and 'abrir' out loud in the pretérito perfeito, all persons.
Say five things you did yesterday, each starting with 'Ontem' and a different regular verb.
Take five present-tense sentences about your routine and move them into the past.

items: Recall the 'eu' endings of the three conjugations: -ei, -i, -i.
Say the difference between 'falamos' (present) and 'falámos' (past) in European Portuguese.
Translate: 'I ate a sandwich and drank a coffee yesterday.'

activities.title: Os verbos regulares no passado
activities.intro: The pretérito perfeito simples reports completed past events. Learn one paradigm per conjugation and reuse it everywhere.

items.hr: Ontem falei com a minha avó ao telefone.
items.en: Yesterday I spoke with my grandmother on the phone.
items.note: falar: falei, falaste, falou, falámos, falaram

items.hr: Tu falaste com o professor?
items.en: Did you speak with the teacher?
items.note: -aste is the 'tu' ending of -AR verbs

items.hr: Nós falámos disso na semana passada.
items.en: We spoke about that last week.
items.note: European Portuguese: falámos (past, with acute accent) vs falamos (present)

items.hr: Comi uma sandes ao almoço.
items.en: I ate a sandwich at lunch.
items.note: comer: comi, comeste, comeu, comemos, comeram

items.hr: Ela bebeu um galão no café da esquina.
items.en: She drank a galão (milky coffee) at the corner café.
items.note: -eu is the 'ele/ela' ending of -ER verbs

items.hr: Abri a janela porque estava calor.
items.en: I opened the window because it was hot.
items.note: abrir: abri, abriste, abriu, abrimos, abriram

items.hr: Eles partiram cedo para o Porto.
items.en: They left early for Porto.
items.note: -iram is the 'eles' ending of -IR verbs

activities.title: Ontem, no passado
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct pretérito perfeito form of each regular verb.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Ontem eu ___ com os meus pais.' (falar)
questions.explanation: 'Eu' with an -AR verb takes -ei in the pretérito perfeito: falei.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Tu ___ o jantar todo?' (comer)
questions.explanation: 'Tu' with an -ER verb takes -este: comeste.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'trabalhar' for 'ele': 'O meu pai ___ no sábado.'
questions.explanation: 'Ele' with an -AR verb takes -ou: trabalhou.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'abrir' for 'nós': 'Nós ___ a loja às nove.'
questions.explanation: 'Nós' with an -IR verb takes -imos: abrimos.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct 'nós' PAST form of 'falar' in European Portuguese?
questions.explanation: European Portuguese marks the past 'nós' form of -AR verbs with an acute accent: falámos (falamos is the present).

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Yesterday I bought bread at the bakery.'
questions.explanation: Time marker first, then verb in the pretérito perfeito: Ontem comprei pão na padaria.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'beber' for 'eles': 'Eles ___ sumo de laranja ao pequeno-almoço.'
questions.explanation: 'Eles' with an -ER verb takes -eram: beberam.

activities.title: O dia de ontem
activities.intro: A friend asks about your day yesterday. Play 'Me' and answer with regular verbs in the pretérito perfeito.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Olá! Trabalhaste ontem?
lines.en: Hi! Did you work yesterday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trabalhei de manhã e depois almocei com a minha irmã.
lines.en: I worked in the morning and then had lunch with my sister.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que bom! E o que comeram?
lines.en: Nice! And what did you eat?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comemos peixe grelhado e bebemos água fresca.
lines.en: We ate grilled fish and drank cold water.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E à noite, ficaste em casa?
lines.en: And in the evening, did you stay at home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, estudei um pouco e jantei tarde.
lines.en: Yes, I studied a little and had dinner late.

days.title: Pretérito perfeito II
days.objective: Master the core irregular verbs in the pretérito perfeito: ser/ir, ter, estar, fazer, dizer, poder, pôr, ver, vir.
days.paretoFocus: Nine irregular verbs cover most past-tense sentences in real conversation, and ser and ir share exactly the same forms (fui, foste, foi...).
drills: Recite 'fui, foste, foi, fomos, foram' and give one 'ser' sentence and one 'ir' sentence with 'foi'.
Say what you did last weekend using at least four irregular verbs (fiz, tive, estive, vi...).
Quiz yourself: 'eu' forms of ter, estar, fazer, dizer, poder, pôr, ver, vir, out loud.

items: Recall: which two verbs have identical pretérito perfeito forms?
Say the 'eu' forms: tive, estive, fiz, disse, pude, pus, vi, vim.
Translate: 'I went to the market and saw a friend.'

activities.title: Os irregulares essenciais
activities.intro: These verbs change their stem in the past. Learn the 'eu' form first, the rest follows a pattern.

items.hr: Ontem fui ao mercado.
items.en: Yesterday I went to the market.
items.note: ir: fui, foste, foi, fomos, foram

items.hr: A viagem foi fantástica.
items.en: The trip was fantastic.
items.note: ser has THE SAME forms as ir, context tells you which verb it is

items.hr: Tive uma reunião longa de manhã.
items.en: I had a long meeting in the morning.
items.note: ter: tive, tiveste, teve, tivemos, tiveram

items.hr: Estivemos em Lisboa no fim de semana.
items.en: We were in Lisbon at the weekend.
items.note: estar: estive, estiveste, esteve, estivemos, estiveram

items.hr: Fiz o jantar para toda a família.
items.en: I made dinner for the whole family.
items.note: fazer: fiz, fizeste, fez, fizemos, fizeram

items.hr: Ele disse que sim.
items.en: He said yes.
items.note: dizer: disse, disseste, disse, dissemos, disseram

items.hr: Não pude ir à festa.
items.en: I couldn't go to the party.
items.note: poder: pude, pudeste, pôde, pudemos, puderam

items.hr: Pus as chaves em cima da mesa.
items.en: I put the keys on the table.
items.note: pôr: pus, puseste, pôs, pusemos, puseram

items.hr: Vi um filme ótimo e depois vim para casa.
items.en: I saw a great film and then came home.
items.note: ver → vi; vir → vim, don't mix them up!

activities.title: Irregulares em ação
activities.intro: Complete each sentence with the correct irregular past form.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Ontem nós ___ ao cinema.' (ir)
questions.explanation: 'Nós' with 'ir' in the past is fomos.

questions.prompt: In 'A festa foi ótima.' and 'Ela foi ao Porto.', the verb 'foi' comes from...
options: ser in the first, ir in the second
questions.answer: ser in the first, ir in the second
questions.explanation: Ser and ir share the same past forms: 'foi ótima' = was (ser); 'foi ao Porto' = went (ir).

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'fazer' for 'tu': 'O que ___ no sábado?'
questions.explanation: 'Tu' with 'fazer' is fizeste, the key question for any weekend chat.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'ter' for 'eu': 'Ontem ___ muito trabalho.'
questions.explanation: 'Eu' with 'ter' is tive.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Ele ___ o livro na mochila.' (pôr)
questions.explanation: 'Ele' with 'pôr' is pôs (with circumflex); pus is the 'eu' form.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'ver' for 'eu': '___ o novo filme português no sábado.'
questions.explanation: 'Eu' with 'ver' is vi; vim is from 'vir' (to come).

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'On Sunday we went to the beach with our friends.'
questions.explanation: Time expression first, then the irregular verb: No domingo fomos à praia com os amigos.

activities.title: O que fizeste no fim de semana?
activities.intro: A colleague asks about your weekend. Play 'Me' and answer with irregular past verbs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, o que fizeste no fim de semana?
lines.en: So, what did you do at the weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No sábado fui ao Porto com a minha prima.
lines.en: On Saturday I went to Porto with my cousin.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que bom! E como foi a viagem?
lines.en: Great! And how was the trip?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Foi ótima. Vimos o rio e tivemos tempo para tudo.
lines.en: It was great. We saw the river and had time for everything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estiveram lá os dois dias?
lines.en: Were you there both days?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, no domingo vim para casa e fiz uma sesta.
lines.en: No, on Sunday I came home and took a nap.

days.title: O fim de semana
days.objective: Narrate a full weekend in the pretérito perfeito, anchored by time markers like ontem, anteontem and na semana passada.
days.paretoFocus: A handful of time markers (ontem, anteontem, na semana passada, há + time) plus sequencers (primeiro, depois) turn isolated verbs into a real story.
drills: Tell your last weekend in six sentences, each opening with a different time marker or sequencer.
Say three things using 'há + time': há dois dias, há uma semana, há um mês.
Retell yesterday morning with 'primeiro..., depois..., no fim...'.

items: Recall the difference between 'ontem' and 'anteontem'.
Say aloud: 'na semana passada', 'no mês passado', 'no sábado passado'.
Translate: 'Two days ago I took the train to Lisbon.'

activities.title: Marcadores de tempo do passado
activities.intro: Time markers place your story on a timeline and almost always call for the pretérito perfeito.

items.hr: Ontem à noite jantei fora.
items.en: Last night I ate out.
items.note: ontem = yesterday

items.hr: Anteontem não saí de casa.
items.en: The day before yesterday I didn't leave the house.
items.note: anteontem = the day before yesterday

items.hr: Na semana passada estive doente.
items.en: Last week I was ill.
items.note: na semana passada / no mês passado / no ano passado

items.hr: No sábado passado fomos a Sintra.
items.en: Last Saturday we went to Sintra.
items.note: no + day + passado = last (day)

items.hr: Há dois dias recebi uma carta.
items.en: Two days ago I received a letter.
items.note: há + time = ago

items.hr: Primeiro tomei o pequeno-almoço, depois saí.
items.en: First I had breakfast, then I went out.
items.note: sequencers: primeiro, depois, a seguir, no fim

items.hr: No fim do dia, apanhámos o comboio para casa.
items.en: At the end of the day, we caught the train home.
items.note: apanhar o comboio / o autocarro = to catch the train / the bus

activities.title: Contar o fim de semana
activities.intro: Combine past verbs with the right time markers.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'the day before yesterday'?
questions.explanation: Anteontem = the day before yesterday; ontem = yesterday.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct word: '___ dois meses visitei os meus avós.'
questions.explanation: 'Há + time' means 'ago': há dois meses = two months ago.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'apanhar' for 'nós': 'De manhã ___ o autocarro para o centro.'
questions.explanation: European Portuguese past 'nós' of -AR verbs takes the acute accent: apanhámos.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct expression: '___ passada fui ao teatro.'
questions.explanation: 'Semana' is feminine: na semana passada = last week.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'sair' for 'eu': 'Anteontem não ___ de casa.'
questions.explanation: 'Eu' with 'sair' is saí (with acute accent on the i).

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Last week I went to Coimbra to visit a friend.'
questions.explanation: Time marker + verb + destination + purpose: Na semana passada fui a Coimbra visitar um amigo.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'No domingo de manhã, primeiro tomei o pequeno-almoço e depois ___ o jornal.' (ler)
questions.explanation: A completed event in a sequence takes the pretérito perfeito: li (eu, ler).

activities.title: Um fim de semana calmo
activities.intro: A neighbour asks about your weekend. Play 'Me' and use time markers to structure your story.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia! Como foi o teu fim de semana?
lines.en: Good morning! How was your weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Foi calmo. No sábado de manhã fui ao mercado.
lines.en: It was quiet. On Saturday morning I went to the market.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E à tarde, o que fizeste?
lines.en: And in the afternoon, what did you do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Passeei à beira-rio e comi um gelado.
lines.en: I strolled along the riverside and ate an ice cream.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E no domingo?
lines.en: And on Sunday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No domingo almocei em casa dos meus pais e voltei tarde.
lines.en: On Sunday I had lunch at my parents' house and came back late.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que fim de semana agradável!
lines.en: What a pleasant weekend!

days.title: Imperfeito
days.objective: Form the imperfeito and use it for past habits, states and descriptions.
days.paretoFocus: Two ending sets (-ava for -AR, -ia for -ER/-IR) plus four irregulars (ser, ter, vir, pôr) give you the whole imperfeito.
drills: Conjugate 'morar' and 'comer' in the imperfeito, all persons, out loud.
List five things you always did as a child, each in the imperfeito.
Describe your old bedroom with 'havia' and three imperfeito descriptions.

items: Recall the imperfeito endings for -AR verbs: -ava, -avas, -ava, -ávamos, -avam.
Say the four irregular imperfeito verbs: ser (era), ter (tinha), vir (vinha), pôr (punha).
Translate: 'In the old days we lived near the beach.'

activities.title: Formar o imperfeito
activities.intro: The imperfeito paints past habits and backgrounds. -AR verbs take -ava endings; -ER and -IR verbs take -ia endings.

items.hr: Antigamente morava numa aldeia.
items.en: In the old days I lived in a village.
items.note: morar: morava, moravas, morava, morávamos, moravam

items.hr: Quando era criança, brincava na rua.
items.en: When I was a child, I played in the street.
items.note: ser is irregular: era, eras, era, éramos, eram

items.hr: Nós jogávamos à bola todos os dias.
items.en: We played football every day.
items.note: 'todos os dias' in the past signals a habit → imperfeito

items.hr: Ele comia sopa ao jantar.
items.en: He used to eat soup at dinner.
items.note: -ER/-IR verbs: -ia, -ias, -ia, -íamos, -iam

items.hr: Tínhamos um cão chamado Bobi.
items.en: We had a dog called Bobi.
items.note: ter is irregular: tinha, tinhas, tinha, tínhamos, tinham

items.hr: A minha avó punha sempre flores na mesa.
items.en: My grandmother always put flowers on the table.
items.note: pôr is irregular: punha, punhas, punha, púnhamos, punham

items.hr: Os meus primos vinham cá no verão.
items.en: My cousins used to come here in the summer.
items.note: vir is irregular: vinha, vinhas, vinha, vínhamos, vinham

activities.title: Hábitos de antigamente
activities.intro: Complete each sentence with the correct imperfeito form.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Quando ___ pequena, a Rita vivia no Algarve.'
questions.explanation: A past state/description uses the imperfeito of 'ser': era.

questions.prompt: Type the imperfeito of 'morar' for 'eu': 'Antigamente ___ perto da praia.'
questions.explanation: -AR verbs take -ava in the imperfeito: morava.

questions.prompt: Type the imperfeito of 'ter' for 'nós': 'Naquela altura ___ menos dinheiro.'
questions.explanation: 'Ter' is irregular in the imperfeito: tínhamos (with acute accent).

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Antigamente, aos domingos, eles ___ os avós.'
questions.explanation: 'Antigamente' + repeated habit ('aos domingos') calls for the imperfeito: visitavam.

questions.prompt: Type the imperfeito of 'pôr' for 'ela': 'A mãe ___ a mesa todas as noites.'
questions.explanation: 'Pôr' is irregular in the imperfeito: punha.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'When I was a child, I used to play in the garden.'
questions.explanation: Two imperfeitos: 'era' (state) and 'brincava' (habit).

questions.prompt: Which set shows the correct imperfeito endings for -ER verbs?
questions.explanation: -ER and -IR verbs share the -ia endings; -ava belongs to -AR verbs; the third set is the pretérito perfeito.

activities.title: Como era antigamente
activities.intro: A friend asks about your past. Play 'Me' and describe habits with the imperfeito.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onde é que moravas antigamente?
lines.en: Where did you live in the old days?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Morava numa aldeia pequena no norte.
lines.en: I lived in a small village in the north.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E como era a tua casa?
lines.en: And what was your house like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Era antiga e tinha um quintal enorme.
lines.en: It was old and had a huge backyard.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O que fazias nas férias?
lines.en: What did you do during the holidays?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ia à ribeira com os meus primos e nadávamos todo o dia.
lines.en: I went to the stream with my cousins and we swam all day.

days.title: Perfeito vs imperfeito
days.objective: Choose between the pretérito perfeito for completed events and the imperfeito for background description within one narrative.
days.paretoFocus: One question decides almost every past-tense choice: is it a finished event (perfeito) or the scenery around it (imperfeito)?
drills: Describe this morning with three perfeito actions inside an imperfeito setting.
Take five sentences and split each into a background clause (imperfeito) and an event clause (perfeito).
Retell what happened when someone knocked while you were cooking, using 'quando' + both tenses.

items: Recall: which tense marks a sudden, completed event in the past?
Say the pattern out loud: 'Estava a + infinitivo... quando + perfeito.'
Translate: 'It was raining when I left the house.'

activities.title: Evento ou cenário?
activities.intro: The perfeito pushes the story forward (what happened); the imperfeito paints what was going on around it.

items.hr: Estava a chover quando saí de casa.
items.en: It was raining when I left the house.
items.note: European Portuguese: estar a + infinitivo for ongoing actions

items.hr: Nós jantávamos quando o telefone tocou.
items.en: We were having dinner when the phone rang.
items.note: background (jantávamos) interrupted by an event (tocou)

items.hr: Ela estava cansada, por isso foi para a cama cedo.
items.en: She was tired, so she went to bed early.
items.note: state = imperfeito, action = perfeito

items.hr: O sol brilhava e as crianças brincavam no parque.
items.en: The sun was shining and the children were playing in the park.
items.note: two parallel descriptions, both imperfeito

items.hr: De repente, um cão atravessou a rua.
items.en: Suddenly, a dog crossed the street.
items.note: 'de repente' signals a perfeito event

items.hr: Enquanto eu lia, o meu irmão via televisão.
items.en: While I was reading, my brother was watching TV.
items.note: 'enquanto' links two simultaneous backgrounds

items.hr: Ontem fui ao médico porque me doía a cabeça.
items.en: Yesterday I went to the doctor because my head was aching.
items.note: event (fui) + ongoing state (doía)

activities.title: Escolher o tempo certo
activities.intro: Decide whether each gap is a completed event or a background description.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Quando eu ___ à paragem, o autocarro partiu.' (chegar)
questions.explanation: Two completed events in sequence: cheguei... partiu, both perfeito.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: '___ muito frio quando saímos.'
questions.explanation: Weather as background description takes the imperfeito: estava frio.

questions.prompt: Type the imperfeito of 'dormir' for 'eles': 'As crianças ___ quando os pais chegaram.'
questions.explanation: Ongoing background action = imperfeito: dormiam.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'tocar' for 'ele': 'Nós víamos um filme quando o telefone ___.'
questions.explanation: The interrupting event takes the perfeito: tocou.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct pair: 'Ela ___ na cozinha quando alguém ___ à porta.'
questions.explanation: Background (estava) + sudden event (bateu).

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'I was reading when you arrived.'
questions.explanation: Background action (lia, imperfeito) interrupted by an event (chegaste, perfeito).

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the past tenses correctly?
options: Ontem fui ao cinema e vi um filme.
Ontem eu ia ao cinema e via um filme.
Ontem fui ao cinema e via um filme.
questions.answer: Ontem fui ao cinema e vi um filme.
questions.explanation: Two completed events yesterday: fui and vi, both pretérito perfeito.

activities.title: Uma manhã complicada
activities.intro: You arrived late and explain why. Play 'Me' and mix background (imperfeito) with events (perfeito).

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O que aconteceu? Chegaste tarde ao trabalho.
lines.en: What happened? You arrived late to work.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois foi. Estava a tomar banho quando faltou a luz.
lines.en: Yes, indeed. I was taking a shower when the power went out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sério? E depois?
lines.en: Really? And then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Saí de casa à pressa, mas chovia imenso.
lines.en: I left the house in a hurry, but it was raining heavily.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Apanhaste o autocarro?
lines.en: Did you catch the bus?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, perdi o autocarro porque havia muito trânsito.
lines.en: No, I missed the bus because there was a lot of traffic.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que manhã complicada!
lines.en: What a complicated morning!

days.title: A infância
days.objective: Talk about childhood memories with the imperfeito and the structure 'costumava + infinitive'.
days.paretoFocus: 'Costumava + infinitive' is a ready-made 'used to' machine, combine it with era/tinha/havia and you can describe any childhood.
drills: Say five things you used to do as a child with 'costumava + infinitive'.
Describe your primary school: where it was, what it had, what you did there, all in the imperfeito.
Contrast then and now: 'Antigamente... , agora...' in three sentences.

items: Recall the structure: costumava / costumavas / costumávamos + infinitive.
Say aloud: 'havia' = there was/were (imperfeito of haver).
Translate: 'When I was little, I was afraid of the dark.'

activities.title: Memórias de infância
activities.intro: Childhood talk lives in the imperfeito: states, habits, and 'costumava + infinitive' for 'used to'.

items.hr: Em criança, costumava passar as férias na praia.
items.en: As a child, I used to spend the holidays at the beach.
items.note: costumava + infinitive = used to

items.hr: Costumávamos jantar em casa dos avós ao domingo.
items.en: We used to have dinner at our grandparents' on Sundays.
items.note: costumávamos = we used to

items.hr: A escola ficava perto de casa e eu ia a pé.
items.en: The school was near home and I walked there.
items.note: ficava = was located (description)

items.hr: Nessa altura não havia telemóveis.
items.en: At that time there were no mobile phones.
items.note: havia = there was/were; telemóvel = mobile phone (pt-PT)

items.hr: O meu avô contava histórias antes de dormir.
items.en: My grandfather told stories before bedtime.
items.note: repeated habit → imperfeito

items.hr: Tinha medo do escuro quando era pequeno.
items.en: I was afraid of the dark when I was little.
items.note: ter medo de = to be afraid of

items.hr: Brincávamos às escondidas no quintal.
items.en: We played hide-and-seek in the backyard.
items.note: brincar às escondidas = to play hide-and-seek

activities.title: Quando era criança
activities.intro: Practise childhood habits with the imperfeito and 'costumava'.

questions.prompt: Type the imperfeito of 'costumar' for 'eu': 'Em criança, ___ acordar cedo.'
questions.explanation: 'Costumava + infinitive' expresses a past habit: I used to wake up early.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Antigamente, nós ___ à bola depois das aulas.'
questions.explanation: A repeated habit in the past takes the imperfeito: jogávamos.

questions.prompt: Choose the European Portuguese word for 'mobile phone':
questions.explanation: In Portugal a mobile phone is a telemóvel; portátil is a laptop.

questions.prompt: Type the imperfeito of 'haver' for 'ele': 'Naquela época não ___ internet.'
questions.explanation: 'Havia' = there was/were, the imperfeito of haver.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'When I was little, I used to play in the backyard.'
questions.explanation: 'Quando era pequeno' sets the frame; 'costumava brincar' gives the habit.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'A minha avó ___ bolos todos os sábados.'
questions.explanation: 'Todos os sábados' marks a repeated past habit: fazia (imperfeito).

questions.prompt: Type the imperfeito of 'ir' for 'nós': 'No verão ___ sempre para o Algarve.'
questions.explanation: 'Ir' in the imperfeito is regular from the -IR pattern: íamos.

activities.title: A tua infância
activities.intro: A friend asks about your childhood. Play 'Me' and answer with the imperfeito and 'costumava'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Como era a tua vida quando eras criança?
lines.en: What was your life like when you were a child?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Era simples. Morávamos numa vila pequena.
lines.en: It was simple. We lived in a small town.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O que costumavas fazer depois da escola?
lines.en: What did you use to do after school?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Costumava brincar na rua com os vizinhos até ao jantar.
lines.en: I used to play in the street with the neighbours until dinner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E não tinhas televisão?
lines.en: And didn't you have a TV?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tínhamos, mas eu preferia andar de bicicleta.
lines.en: We did, but I preferred riding my bike.

days.title: Pronomes de complemento direto
days.objective: Use the direct object pronouns o/a/os/as with ênclise, the -lo/-la forms after -r and -s, and próclise after negation.
days.paretoFocus: European Portuguese hangs the pronoun AFTER the verb by default (vejo-a); learn the -r/-s → -lo/-la sound rule and the 'não' exception and you sound native.
drills: Replace the object with a pronoun in five sentences: 'Vejo o João' → 'Vejo-o', etc.
Practise the infinitive rule out loud: comprar+o → comprá-lo, ver+a → vê-la, abrir+os → abri-los.
Flip five affirmative sentences into the negative and move the pronoun: 'Vejo-a' → 'Não a vejo'.

items: Recall: where does the pronoun go in a normal affirmative sentence in pt-PT?
Say the transformation: comprar + o = comprá-lo (the -r drops, accent appears).
Translate: 'I don't see her anymore.'

activities.title: Vejo-a, comprei-o
activities.intro: The direct object pronouns are o, a, os, as. In European Portuguese they attach to the END of the verb with a hyphen, unless a trigger word pulls them in front.

items.hr: Vejo-a todos os dias no café.
items.en: I see her every day at the café.
items.note: ênclise: verb-hyphen-pronoun is the default in pt-PT

items.hr: Compraste o jornal? Sim, comprei-o de manhã.
items.en: Did you buy the newspaper? Yes, I bought it in the morning.
items.note: o = it/him

items.hr: Adoro estas fotografias. Guardo-as no telemóvel.
items.en: I love these photos. I keep them on my phone.
items.note: as = them (feminine)

items.hr: Vou comprá-lo amanhã.
items.en: I'm going to buy it tomorrow.
items.note: after -r the pronoun becomes -lo/-la: comprar + o → comprá-lo

items.hr: Queres ver o filme? Podemos vê-lo hoje à noite.
items.en: Do you want to see the film? We can watch it tonight.
items.note: ver + o → vê-lo

items.hr: Tu fazes o bolo e nós comemo-lo.
items.en: You make the cake and we eat it.
items.note: after -s the pronoun also becomes -lo: comemos + o → comemo-lo

items.hr: Não a vejo desde segunda-feira.
items.en: I haven't seen her since Monday.
items.note: negation triggers próclise: the pronoun moves BEFORE the verb

activities.title: Onde fica o pronome?
activities.intro: Attach, transform or move the direct object pronoun correctly.

questions.prompt: Replace the object: 'Vejo a Ana todos os dias.' →
questions.explanation: Affirmative main clause → ênclise: vejo-a. The -la form only appears after -r or -s.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Vou comprar o bilhete.' → 'Vou ___ amanhã.'
questions.explanation: The infinitive drops its -r and the pronoun becomes -lo: comprá-lo.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.explanation: 'Não' forces próclise: the pronoun goes before the verb, não o vejo.

questions.prompt: Rewrite with a pronoun: 'Vou levar as chaves.' → 'Vou ___.' Type only the verb with the pronoun attached.
questions.explanation: After an infinitive the -r drops and the pronoun becomes -las: levar + as → levá-las.

questions.prompt: Rewrite with a pronoun: 'Queremos ver o museu.' Type only the infinitive with the pronoun attached.
questions.explanation: Ver + o: the -r drops and the pronoun becomes -lo, with a circumflex, vê-lo.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'I haven't seen them since yesterday.'
questions.explanation: Negation pulls the pronoun in front of the verb: Não os vejo.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Fazes tu o jantar?', 'Sim, faço-___ eu.'
questions.explanation: 'Faço' ends in a vowel, so the plain pronoun attaches: faço-o.

activities.title: Já o viste?
activities.intro: You and a friend talk about a book, a film and concert tickets. Play 'Me' and use direct object pronouns.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já leste o livro que te emprestei?
lines.en: Have you read the book I lent you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda não o li todo, mas estou a gostar muito.
lines.en: I haven't read it all yet, but I'm enjoying it a lot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o filme novo? Viste-o?
lines.en: And the new film? Did you see it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vi-o no sábado. Adorei!
lines.en: I saw it on Saturday. I loved it!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tenho os bilhetes para o concerto. Queres vê-los?
lines.en: I have the tickets for the concert. Do you want to see them?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Claro! Mostra-os lá!
lines.en: Of course! Come on, show them!

days.title: Pronomes de complemento indireto
days.objective: Use the indirect object pronouns me/te/lhe/nos/lhes with ênclise, and recognise the combined forms mo/to/lho.
days.paretoFocus: 'Lhe' and 'lhes' replace 'a + person' in most everyday sentences, master 'dou-lhe' and 'telefonei-lhes' and you cover giving, telling, asking and phoning.
drills: Say five sentences with 'lhe': dar, dizer, pedir, telefonar, escrever + lhe.
Replace the person in five sentences: 'Escrevi à Maria' → 'Escrevi-lhe'.
Read 'dou-to' and 'emprestei-lho' out loud and unpack each into pronoun + pronoun.

items: Recall the indirect pronouns: me, te, lhe, nos, lhes.
Say what 'lho' stands for: lhe + o.
Translate: 'I phoned them last night.'

activities.title: Dou-lhe, telefonei-lhes
activities.intro: Indirect object pronouns replace 'to someone'. Like direct pronouns, they attach after the verb by default in European Portuguese.

items.hr: Dou-lhe o livro amanhã.
items.en: I'll give him/her the book tomorrow.
items.note: lhe = to him / to her / to you (formal)

items.hr: Telefonei-lhes ontem à noite.
items.en: I phoned them last night.
items.note: lhes = to them

items.hr: Ela deu-me um presente lindo.
items.en: She gave me a beautiful present.
items.note: me = to me

items.hr: Escrevo-te um e-mail logo.
items.en: I'll write you an e-mail later.
items.note: te = to you (informal)

items.hr: O empregado trouxe-nos a conta.
items.en: The waiter brought us the bill.
items.note: nos = to us

items.hr: Não lhe disse nada.
items.en: I didn't tell him/her anything.
items.note: negation → próclise, same rule as direct pronouns

items.hr: Dás-me o livro? Dou-to já.
items.en: Will you give me the book? I'll give it to you right away.
items.note: combined form: te + o = to (just recognise it for now)

items.hr: Ela pediu-me o carro e eu emprestei-lho.
items.en: She asked me for the car and I lent it to her.
items.note: combined form: lhe + o = lho

activities.title: A quem? Lhe ou lhes?
activities.intro: Replace the person with the right indirect pronoun and put it in the right place.

questions.prompt: Replace the person: 'Dei um presente à Maria.' →
questions.explanation: 'À Maria' is an indirect object (to Maria), so it becomes lhe: dei-lhe.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Escreveste aos teus avós?', 'Sim, escrevi-___ ontem.'
questions.explanation: 'Aos teus avós' is plural: lhes = to them.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pronoun meaning 'to us': 'O guia mostrou-___ a cidade.'
questions.explanation: Mostrou-nos = he showed us (to us) the city.

questions.prompt: In 'Dou-to amanhã.', the form 'to' stands for:
questions.explanation: Combined pronouns merge: te + o = to → 'I'll give it to you tomorrow.'

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Negation forces próclise: não lhe telefonei.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pronoun meaning 'to her': 'Pedi-___ ajuda ontem.'
questions.explanation: Pedi-lhe ajuda = I asked her for help; lhe covers him, her and formal you.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'The waiter brought us the bill.'
questions.explanation: Affirmative sentence → ênclise: trouxe-nos.

activities.title: Presentes para a família
activities.intro: You discuss presents for your parents. Play 'Me' and use indirect pronouns, one combined form included.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já compraste o presente para a tua mãe?
lines.en: Have you already bought the present for your mum?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Já. Vou dar-lho no domingo.
lines.en: Yes. I'm going to give it to her on Sunday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o que lhe compraste?
lines.en: And what did you buy her?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comprei-lhe um lenço azul.
lines.en: I bought her a blue scarf.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ela vai adorar. E ao teu pai?
lines.en: She's going to love it. And for your dad?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vou oferecer-lhe um livro de história. Ele disse-me que queria um.
lines.en: I'm going to give him a history book. He told me he wanted one.

days.title: Ênclise e próclise
days.objective: Place clitic pronouns correctly: ênclise by default, próclise after negation, question words, 'que' clauses and adverbs like já, ainda, também, nunca.
days.paretoFocus: Memorise the short trigger list (não, nunca, quem/o que, que, já, ainda, também), everything else defaults to ênclise.
drills: Take 'Vejo-te amanhã' and rebuild it after each trigger: não, nunca, já, também.
Ask three questions with 'Quem te...?' / 'O que lhe...?' out loud.
Say five 'que' sentences: 'Ele disse que me viu', 'Acho que a conheço'...

items: Recite the próclise triggers: não, nunca, question words, que, já, ainda, também.
Recall: what is the default position of the pronoun in European Portuguese?
Translate: 'I have already read it.'

activities.title: Depois ou antes do verbo?
activities.intro: European Portuguese defaults to ênclise (verb-pronoun), but certain 'magnet' words pull the pronoun in front of the verb (próclise).

items.hr: Vejo-te amanhã.
items.en: I'll see you tomorrow.
items.note: default: ênclise (pronoun after the verb)

items.hr: Não te vejo amanhã.
items.en: I won't see you tomorrow.
items.note: não → próclise

items.hr: Quem te disse isso?
items.en: Who told you that?
items.note: question words → próclise

items.hr: Ele disse que me conhece.
items.en: He said that he knows me.
items.note: 'que' clauses → próclise

items.hr: Já o li.
items.en: I have already read it.
items.note: já → próclise

items.hr: Ainda não os encontrei.
items.en: I still haven't found them.
items.note: ainda (não) → próclise

items.hr: Eu também a vi no cinema.
items.en: I also saw her at the cinema.
items.note: também → próclise

items.hr: Nunca lhe contei o segredo.
items.en: I never told him/her the secret.
items.note: nunca → próclise

activities.title: O íman do pronome
activities.intro: Spot the trigger word and place the pronoun on the right side of the verb.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence for 'I have already seen it':
questions.explanation: 'Já' is a próclise trigger: the pronoun goes before the verb.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct word order for 'I know her well' (affirmative main clause):
questions.explanation: No trigger word → default ênclise: conheço-a.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ele disse que ___ ontem.' (he saw me yesterday)
questions.explanation: Inside a 'que' clause the pronoun moves before the verb: que me viu.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'dizer' for 'ele': 'Foi ele que me ___ a verdade.'
questions.explanation: 'Que' triggers próclise (me before the verb); dizer for 'ele' is disse.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'encontrar' for 'eu': 'Ainda não o ___.'
questions.explanation: 'Ainda não' pulls the pronoun forward; the verb is encontrei.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'I didn't tell him anything.'
questions.explanation: Negation → próclise: Não lhe disse nada.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Who told you that story?'
questions.explanation: Question word → próclise: Quem te contou...?

activities.title: Onde está o casaco?
activities.intro: A flatmate is looking for lost things. Play 'Me' and watch the pronoun placement in each line.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Viste o meu casaco? Não o encontro.
lines.en: Have you seen my coat? I can't find it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vi-o na cozinha, em cima da cadeira.
lines.en: I saw it in the kitchen, on the chair.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Obrigado! E as minhas luvas? Também as viste?
lines.en: Thanks! And my gloves? Did you see them too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, não as vi. Já procuraste no carro?
lines.en: No, I didn't see them. Have you already looked in the car?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ainda não. Perco sempre tudo!
lines.en: Not yet. I always lose everything!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Calma, eu ajudo-te a procurar.
lines.en: Relax, I'll help you look.

days.title: Revisão A2 I
days.objective: Consolidate the past tenses (perfeito and imperfeito) and clitic pronoun placement in one mixed review.
days.paretoFocus: The 'how was your weekend?' conversation recycles everything from this block: perfeito events, imperfeito backgrounds and pronouns in the right place.
drills: Tell your last weekend in eight sentences mixing perfeito and imperfeito.
Answer out loud: 'Já leste o jornal? Telefonaste à tua mãe?', with pronouns (Já o li / Telefonei-lhe).
Describe your childhood in three sentences, then say what you did yesterday in three more.

items: Recall: perfeito = completed event; imperfeito = habit, state, background.
Recite the próclise triggers one more time: não, nunca, quem, que, já, ainda, também.
Translate: 'I was reading when she arrived, and I told her everything.'

activities.title: Tudo junto: o passado e os pronomes
activities.intro: One recap sentence per pattern from lessons 1-9. If any feels shaky, revisit that day.

items.hr: Ontem fui ao ginásio e depois jantei com amigos.
items.en: Yesterday I went to the gym and then had dinner with friends.
items.note: perfeito for completed events in sequence

items.hr: Quando era estudante, vivia em Coimbra.
items.en: When I was a student, I lived in Coimbra.
items.note: imperfeito for past states and habits

items.hr: Estava a ler quando ela chegou.
items.en: I was reading when she arrived.
items.note: estar a + infinitivo (background) + perfeito (event)

items.hr: Compraste as maçãs? Comprei-as no mercado.
items.en: Did you buy the apples? I bought them at the market.
items.note: direct pronoun with ênclise

items.hr: Telefonaste à tua mãe? Sim, telefonei-lhe de manhã.
items.en: Did you phone your mum? Yes, I phoned her in the morning.
items.note: indirect pronoun lhe

items.hr: Ainda não lhes respondi.
items.en: I still haven't answered them.
items.note: 'ainda não' → próclise

items.hr: Costumávamos ir à praia todos os verões.
items.en: We used to go to the beach every summer.
items.note: costumava + infinitive = used to

activities.title: Grande revisão
activities.intro: Mixed questions from the whole block, tenses and pronouns together.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'No sábado passado nós ___ uma caminhada na serra.' (fazer)
questions.explanation: A single completed event last Saturday takes the perfeito: fizemos.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Antigamente, o meu pai ___ no centro da cidade.'
questions.explanation: 'Antigamente' signals a past habit: imperfeito trabalhava.

questions.prompt: Type the pretérito perfeito of 'vir' for 'eu': 'Ontem ___ para casa de comboio.'
questions.explanation: 'Vir' for 'eu' in the past is vim (vi is from 'ver').

questions.prompt: Type the imperfeito of 'ser' for 'nós': 'Naquele tempo ___ muito felizes.'
questions.explanation: 'Ser' in the imperfeito: era, eras, era, éramos, eram.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.explanation: 'Já' triggers próclise: já lhe dei.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Onde está o pão?', '___ esta manhã.' (I ate it)
questions.explanation: Affirmative answer with no trigger → ênclise: comi-o.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'On Sunday I stayed at home because it was raining.'
questions.explanation: Event (fiquei, perfeito) + background (estava a chover, imperfeito).

activities.title: Como foi o teu fim de semana?
activities.intro: The classic Monday conversation. Play 'Me' and combine both past tenses and a pronoun or two.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia! Então, como foi o teu fim de semana?
lines.en: Good morning! So, how was your weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Foi ótimo! No sábado fui a Cascais com uns amigos.
lines.en: It was great! On Saturday I went to Cascais with some friends.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que inveja! Estava bom tempo?
lines.en: I'm so jealous! Was the weather good?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estava sol, mas o mar estava frio. Mesmo assim, nadámos um bocadinho.
lines.en: It was sunny, but the sea was cold. Even so, we swam a little.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E no domingo, o que fizeste?
lines.en: And on Sunday, what did you do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dormi até tarde e li um livro. Comecei-o há uma semana e já o acabei.
lines.en: I slept in and read a book. I started it a week ago and I've already finished it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfeito! Eu fiquei em casa porque tinha muito trabalho.
lines.en: Perfect! I stayed at home because I had a lot of work.

days.title: No médico
days.objective: Book a medical appointment and describe basic symptoms using 'doer' with indirect pronouns (dói-me / doem-me).
days.paretoFocus: One pattern ('dói-me + singular' vs 'doem-me + plural') lets you report almost any pain, and 'queria marcar uma consulta' opens every appointment call.
drills: Say three real or invented complaints out loud: one with 'dói-me', one with 'doem-me', one with 'tenho'.
Role-play the first 20 seconds of a phone call to the centro de saúde: greet, ask to book, give your name.
Answer the question 'O que é que lhe dói?' with three different body-pain sentences.

items: Recall: which form of 'doer' goes with 'as costas', dói or doem?
Say the polite opener for booking anything: 'Queria marcar...'.
Translate: 'My head hurts and I have a cough.'

activities.title: Marcar consulta e descrever sintomas
activities.intro: In Portugal you book a 'consulta' at the 'centro de saúde' or a private clinic. Pain is expressed with 'doer' + indirect pronoun: the body part is the subject.

items.hr: Queria marcar uma consulta para amanhã, por favor.
items.en: I'd like to book an appointment for tomorrow, please.
items.note: 'queria' softens the request, more polite than 'quero'

items.hr: Dói-me a cabeça desde ontem.
items.en: My head has been hurting since yesterday.
items.note: singular subject (a cabeça) -> dói-me

items.hr: Doem-me as costas quando me levanto.
items.en: My back hurts when I get up.
items.note: 'as costas' is plural in Portuguese -> doem-me

items.hr: O que é que lhe dói?
items.en: What hurts (you)?
items.note: the doctor's standard question; 'lhe' = formal you

items.hr: Tenho tosse e sinto-me muito cansado.
items.en: I have a cough and I feel very tired.
items.note: sentir-se = to feel (reflexive)

items.hr: O médico vai receitar-lhe um medicamento.
items.en: The doctor is going to prescribe you a medicine.
items.note: receitar = to prescribe

items.hr: A consulta é às dez e meia no centro de saúde.
items.en: The appointment is at half past ten at the health centre.
items.note: centro de saúde = public health centre in Portugal

activities.title: Dói-me ou doem-me?
activities.intro: Choose the right form of 'doer' and build appointment sentences.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: '___ a garganta.'
questions.explanation: 'A garganta' is singular, so the verb is singular: dói-me a garganta.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: '___ as pernas depois do ginásio.'
questions.explanation: 'As pernas' is plural, so the verb is plural: doem-me as pernas.

questions.prompt: Where do you normally go for a public-system appointment in Portugal?
questions.explanation: Routine consultations happen at the 'centro de saúde', the local public health centre.

questions.prompt: Type the verb that means 'to book' an appointment: 'Queria ___ uma consulta.'
questions.explanation: 'Marcar uma consulta' is the fixed expression for booking an appointment.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'doer': '___-me os olhos de ler no telemóvel.'
questions.explanation: 'Os olhos' is plural, so 'doer' takes the plural form: doem-me os olhos.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I'd like to book an appointment for Monday.'
questions.explanation: Polite 'queria' + infinitive + object + time expression at the end.

activities.title: Ao telefone com o centro de saúde
activities.intro: You call to book an appointment. Play 'Me' and give your symptoms briefly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Centro de saúde da Graça, bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.en: Graça health centre, good morning. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Queria marcar uma consulta, se possível para hoje.
lines.en: Good morning. I'd like to book an appointment, if possible for today.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com certeza. Pode dizer-me o que sente?
lines.en: Of course. Can you tell me what you're feeling?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dói-me muito a cabeça e tenho tosse desde ontem.
lines.en: My head hurts a lot and I've had a cough since yesterday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está bem. Temos uma vaga às quatro da tarde com a doutora Silva.
lines.en: All right. We have a slot at four in the afternoon with Dr Silva.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito, fico com essa. Muito obrigado, até logo.
lines.en: Perfect, I'll take that one. Thank you very much, see you later.

days.title: O corpo e as dores
days.objective: Name the main parts of the body and describe common health complaints: estar constipado, ter febre, ter dores de...
days.paretoFocus: Ten body words plus three frames, 'dói-me o/a...', 'tenho dores de...', 'estou constipado', cover nearly every everyday health complaint.
drills: Point at (or think of) ten body parts and name each in Portuguese with its article.
Describe a bad flu day using 'estou constipado', 'tenho febre' and one 'dói-me' sentence.
Contrast out loud: 'estou constipado' (I have a cold) vs 'tenho prisão de ventre', remember 'constipado' is a false friend.

items: Recall yesterday's rule: dói-me + singular, doem-me + plural.
Say five body parts with the correct article: a cabeça, o braço, a perna, o joelho, as costas.
Translate: 'I have a fever and a sore throat.'

activities.title: Partes do corpo e queixas
activities.intro: Watch out for the classic false friend: in European Portuguese 'estar constipado' means to have a cold, nothing else.

items.hr: a cabeça, o pescoço, a garganta
items.en: the head, the neck, the throat
items.note: top-down order helps memorisation

items.hr: o braço, a mão, o dedo
items.en: the arm, the hand, the finger

items.hr: a perna, o joelho, o pé
items.en: the leg, the knee, the foot

items.hr: Estou constipado: tenho o nariz entupido.
items.en: I have a cold: my nose is blocked.
items.note: false friend! 'constipado' = with a cold, in Portugal

items.hr: Tenho febre: trinta e oito e meio.
items.en: I have a fever: thirty-eight point five.
items.note: ter febre = to have a fever

items.hr: Tenho dores de barriga e não tenho fome.
items.en: I have a stomach ache and I'm not hungry.
items.note: ter dores de + body area

items.hr: Sinto-me tonto e preciso de me sentar.
items.en: I feel dizzy and I need to sit down.
items.note: sentir-se tonto = to feel dizzy

activities.title: Queixas e corpo
activities.intro: Match complaints to situations and recall the body vocabulary.

questions.prompt: 'Estou constipado' means...
questions.explanation: A classic false friend: in Portugal 'estar constipado' = to have a cold.

questions.prompt: You sneeze, your nose is blocked and your throat hurts. What do you say?
questions.explanation: Those are the symptoms of a cold: estou constipado.

questions.prompt: Choose the body part that goes between the leg and the foot in the sequence 'a perna, ___, o pé' (bends when you walk):
questions.explanation: 'O joelho' (the knee) is the joint between the leg and the foot.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Tenho 39 graus, tenho ___.'
questions.explanation: 39 degrees is a fever: tenho febre.

questions.prompt: Type the Portuguese word for 'throat': 'Dói-me a ___.'
questions.explanation: 'A garganta' = the throat; a very common complaint with a cold.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I've had a stomach ache since last night.'
questions.explanation: 'Ter dores de + body area' + 'desde' for the starting point of the pain.

activities.title: Não me sinto bem
activities.intro: A flatmate notices you look ill. Play 'Me' and describe what you feel.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estás com má cara. Sentes-te bem?
lines.en: You don't look well. Do you feel okay?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nem por isso. Acho que estou constipado.
lines.en: Not really. I think I have a cold.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tens febre?
lines.en: Do you have a fever?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tenho, trinta e oito. E dói-me a garganta quando falo.
lines.en: I do, thirty-eight. And my throat hurts when I speak.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então bebe um chá quente e vai descansar. Precisas de alguma coisa?
lines.en: Then drink a hot tea and go rest. Do you need anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Obrigado. Se fores à farmácia, traz-me qualquer coisa para a tosse.
lines.en: Thanks. If you go to the pharmacy, bring me something for the cough.

days.title: Na farmácia
days.objective: Ask for medicine politely with 'podia dar-me...', understand dosage instructions, and know when a 'receita' is needed.
days.paretoFocus: The frame 'Podia dar-me alguma coisa para + problem?' gets you any over-the-counter medicine, and 'de X em X horas' decodes every dosage label.
drills: Ask for three remedies with 'Podia dar-me alguma coisa para...?' (a tosse, as dores de cabeça, a febre).
Read an imaginary label aloud: 'um comprimido de oito em oito horas, depois das refeições'.
Practise the pharmacist's question 'Tem receita médica?' and both answers: 'Tenho, aqui está' / 'Não, não tenho'.

items: Recall three complaint frames from yesterday: dói-me..., tenho dores de..., estou constipado.
Say the polite request opener: 'Podia dar-me...?'
Translate: 'One pill every eight hours.'

activities.title: Medicamentos e dosagem
activities.intro: Portuguese pharmacies (sinal: a green cross) sell many remedies over the counter, but antibiotics need a 'receita médica'. Requests use the polite imperfect 'podia'.

items.hr: Podia dar-me alguma coisa para a tosse?
items.en: Could you give me something for the cough?
items.note: 'podia' (imperfect) = polite 'could you'

items.hr: Precisa de receita médica para este medicamento.
items.en: You need a prescription for this medicine.
items.note: receita (médica) = prescription

items.hr: Tome um comprimido de oito em oito horas.
items.en: Take one pill every eight hours.
items.note: de X em X horas = every X hours

items.hr: Este xarope é para a tosse; tome-o depois das refeições.
items.en: This syrup is for the cough; take it after meals.
items.note: o xarope = syrup

items.hr: Ponha a pomada duas vezes por dia.
items.en: Apply the ointment twice a day.
items.note: a pomada = ointment/cream

items.hr: Tem alguma alergia a medicamentos?
items.en: Do you have any allergy to medicines?
items.note: the pharmacist's safety question

items.hr: Tome o comprimido em jejum, com um copo de água.
items.en: Take the pill on an empty stomach, with a glass of water.
items.note: em jejum = on an empty stomach

activities.title: Pedidos e instruções
activities.intro: Practise polite requests and decode dosage instructions.

questions.prompt: The most polite way to ask for cough medicine is:
options: Podia dar-me alguma coisa para a tosse?
questions.answer: Podia dar-me alguma coisa para a tosse?
questions.explanation: The imperfect 'podia' + 'dar-me alguma coisa para...' is the standard polite request.

questions.prompt: 'Tome um comprimido de seis em seis horas' means:
questions.explanation: 'De X em X horas' expresses the interval between doses: every six hours.

questions.prompt: Which product do you rub on the skin?
questions.explanation: 'A pomada' (ointment) is applied to the skin; syrup is drunk, pills are swallowed.

questions.prompt: Type the document the doctor writes so you can buy antibiotics: a ___ médica.
questions.explanation: 'A receita médica' is the prescription, required for antibiotics in Portugal.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite request: '___ dar-me um xarope para a garganta?'
questions.explanation: 'Podia' (imperfect of poder) softens the request: Podia dar-me...?

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Take one pill every eight hours.'
questions.explanation: Formal imperative 'tome' + quantity + the interval pattern 'de oito em oito horas'.

activities.title: Na farmácia da esquina
activities.intro: You still have that cough. Play 'Me' and get something for it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. O que deseja?
lines.en: Good afternoon. What would you like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Podia dar-me alguma coisa para a tosse?
lines.en: Good afternoon. Could you give me something for the cough?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com certeza. É tosse seca ou com expetoração?
lines.en: Of course. Is it a dry cough or with phlegm?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É seca, principalmente à noite. Também me dói a garganta.
lines.en: It's dry, mainly at night. My throat also hurts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então leve este xarope. Tome uma colher de oito em oito horas.
lines.en: Then take this syrup. Take one spoonful every eight hours.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Preciso de receita? E quanto custa?
lines.en: Do I need a prescription? And how much does it cost?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não precisa. São sete euros e cinquenta.
lines.en: You don't. It's seven euros fifty.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ótimo, levo-o. Obrigado e boa tarde.
lines.en: Great, I'll take it. Thank you and good afternoon.

days.title: Preparar uma viagem
days.objective: Plan and book a trip: reservar, comprar bilhetes, fazer a mala, and check documents before going on holiday.
days.paretoFocus: Four verbs (reservar, comprar, fazer (a mala), levar) plus 'ir de férias' organise the whole pre-trip conversation.
drills: List out loud five things you always put in your suitcase, each with its article.
Say your dream trip in three sentences: where you go, what you book, what you take.
Practise the checklist question 'Já tens...?' with: os bilhetes, o passaporte, a mala.

items: Recall the polite request frame from the pharmacy: 'Podia dar-me...?'
Say three documents you may need to travel: o passaporte, o bilhete, o cartão de cidadão.
Translate: 'I'd like to book a return ticket to Porto.'

activities.title: Reservas, malas e documentos
activities.intro: Trip planning revolves around booking ('reservar'), tickets ('bilhete de ida / de ida e volta') and packing ('fazer a mala').

items.hr: Vamos de férias ao Algarve em agosto.
items.en: We're going on holiday to the Algarve in August.
items.note: ir de férias = to go on holiday

items.hr: Queria reservar um quarto para duas noites.
items.en: I'd like to book a room for two nights.
items.note: reservar = to book

items.hr: Comprei um bilhete de ida e volta para o Porto.
items.en: I bought a return ticket to Porto.
items.note: ida e volta = return; só ida = one-way

items.hr: Ainda tenho de fazer a mala.
items.en: I still have to pack my suitcase.
items.note: fazer a mala = to pack

items.hr: Não te esqueças do passaporte e do cartão de cidadão.
items.en: Don't forget your passport and your citizen card.
items.note: esquecer-se de = to forget (about)

items.hr: Vou levar o carregador do telemóvel e um casaco quente.
items.en: I'm going to take my phone charger and a warm jacket.
items.note: telemóvel = mobile phone in Portugal

items.hr: Reservei tudo pela internet: o voo e o hotel.
items.en: I booked everything online: the flight and the hotel.
items.note: pela internet = online

activities.title: Antes de partir
activities.intro: Booking phrases, packing vocabulary and travel documents.

questions.prompt: You want to travel to Faro and come back. You buy...
options: um bilhete de ida e volta
questions.answer: um bilhete de ida e volta
questions.explanation: 'Ida e volta' = there and back, i.e. a return ticket.

questions.prompt: 'Fazer a mala' means:
questions.explanation: 'Fazer a mala' is the fixed expression for packing.

questions.prompt: Which word is the European Portuguese one for a mobile phone?
questions.explanation: In Portugal a mobile phone is 'o telemóvel'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Queria ___ um quarto duplo para o fim de semana.' (to book)
questions.explanation: 'Reservar' is the verb for booking rooms, tables and tickets.

questions.prompt: Complete the expression for going on holiday: 'Em julho vamos ___ férias.'
questions.explanation: The fixed expression is 'ir de férias', with 'de', not 'em' or 'a'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Don't forget your passport.'
questions.explanation: In a negative command the pronoun goes before the verb: não te esqueças.

activities.title: Planos para as férias
activities.intro: You and a friend finalise a trip. Play 'Me' and confirm the bookings.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, já está tudo pronto para a viagem?
lines.en: So, is everything ready for the trip?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quase. Já reservei o hotel e comprei os bilhetes pela internet.
lines.en: Almost. I've already booked the hotel and bought the tickets online.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa! De ida e volta?
lines.en: Nice! Return tickets?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, de ida e volta. Só falta fazer a mala.
lines.en: Yes, return. I just need to pack.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não te esqueças dos documentos e do carregador do telemóvel.
lines.en: Don't forget your documents and your phone charger.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens razão. Ponho já o passaporte na mochila.
lines.en: You're right. I'll put my passport in the backpack right now.

days.title: No hotel
days.objective: Handle check-in and check-out, ask about the room and breakfast, and complain politely about a problem.
days.paretoFocus: Three moments (chegar (check-in), pedir informações (breakfast, Wi-Fi), reclamar ('não funciona')) cover the entire hotel stay.
drills: Role-play a 30-second check-in: give your name, say you have a reservation, ask about breakfast.
Complain about three problems with '... não funciona': o ar condicionado, o chuveiro, a televisão.
Ask three questions at the receção: A que horas é o pequeno-almoço? Qual é a palavra-passe do Wi-Fi? A que horas é o check-out?

items: Recall the booking verb and one full sentence with it from yesterday.
Say the pt-PT words for breakfast and bathroom: o pequeno-almoço, a casa de banho.
Translate: 'The air conditioning doesn't work.'

activities.title: Do check-in à reclamação
activities.intro: European Portuguese hotel words: 'o pequeno-almoço' (breakfast), 'a casa de banho' (bathroom), 'a receção' (reception). Complaints are polite but direct: 'não funciona'.

items.hr: Tenho uma reserva em nome de Silva.
items.en: I have a reservation under the name Silva.
items.note: em nome de = under the name of

items.hr: Queria um quarto duplo com vista para o mar.
items.en: I'd like a double room with a sea view.
items.note: quarto duplo = double room

items.hr: O pequeno-almoço está incluído no preço?
items.en: Is breakfast included in the price?
items.note: pequeno-almoço = breakfast in Portugal

items.hr: A casa de banho não tem toalhas.
items.en: The bathroom has no towels.
items.note: casa de banho = bathroom in Portugal

items.hr: Desculpe, o ar condicionado não funciona.
items.en: Excuse me, the air conditioning doesn't work.
items.note: 'não funciona' is the key complaint frame

items.hr: A que horas é o check-out? Posso deixar a mala na receção?
items.en: What time is check-out? Can I leave my suitcase at reception?
items.note: a receção = reception (pt-PT spelling)

items.hr: Qual é a palavra-passe do Wi-Fi?
items.en: What is the Wi-Fi password?
items.note: palavra-passe = password in Portugal

activities.title: Na receção
activities.intro: Check-in phrases, hotel vocabulary and polite complaints.

questions.prompt: In Portugal, the morning meal at the hotel is called:
questions.explanation: In European Portuguese breakfast is 'o pequeno-almoço'.

questions.prompt: The shower in your room gives no hot water. You say:
questions.explanation: 'Desculpe, ... não funciona' is the standard polite complaint.

questions.prompt: A room for two people is:
questions.explanation: 'Quarto duplo' = double room; 'quarto individual' = single room.

questions.prompt: Complete the check-in phrase: 'Tenho uma ___ em nome de Costa.'
questions.explanation: 'Tenho uma reserva em nome de...' is how you announce your booking.

questions.prompt: Type the pt-PT word for the room where you shower: a casa de ___.
questions.explanation: In Portugal the bathroom is 'a casa de banho'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Breakfast is included in the price.'
questions.explanation: Yes/no questions keep normal word order in Portuguese, only intonation changes.

activities.title: Chegada ao hotel
activities.intro: You arrive at the hotel and later report a problem. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde, bem-vindo. Tem reserva?
lines.en: Good afternoon, welcome. Do you have a reservation?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Tenho, sim, em nome de Martins. Um quarto duplo para três noites.
lines.en: Good afternoon. Yes I do, under the name Martins. A double room for three nights.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo. Aqui está o cartão do quarto, é o 204. O pequeno-almoço é das sete às dez.
lines.en: Right. Here is your room card, it's 204. Breakfast is from seven to ten.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Obrigado. Qual é a palavra-passe do Wi-Fi?
lines.en: Thank you. What's the Wi-Fi password?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está no cartão. Precisa de mais alguma coisa?
lines.en: It's on the card. Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, desculpe: o ar condicionado do quarto não funciona e a casa de banho não tem toalhas.
lines.en: Yes, excuse me: the air conditioning in the room doesn't work and the bathroom has no towels.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Peço desculpa. Mando já alguém resolver isso.
lines.en: I do apologise. I'll send someone to sort that out right away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Obrigado pela ajuda.
lines.en: Thanks for the help.

days.title: Na estação e no aeroporto
days.objective: Navigate trains and flights: ask about horários, understand delays and platforms, and follow boarding announcements.
days.paretoFocus: Two questions, 'A que horas parte...?' and 'De que linha/porta parte...?', plus recognising 'atrasado' solve most station and airport situations.
drills: Ask about three departures: A que horas parte o comboio para Coimbra? o autocarro? o voo?
React to 'O voo está atrasado uma hora' with a question about the new boarding time.
Say a mini announcement: 'O comboio com destino ao Porto parte da linha três.'

items: Recall three hotel phrases: reserva, pequeno-almoço incluído, não funciona.
Say the pt-PT words for train and bus: o comboio, o autocarro.
Translate: 'The train to Lisbon leaves from platform two.'

activities.title: Partidas e chegadas
activities.intro: In Portugal the train is 'o comboio' and platforms are announced as 'linha'. At the airport, listen for 'porta de embarque' (gate) and 'atrasado' (delayed).

items.hr: A que horas parte o próximo comboio para Coimbra?
items.en: What time does the next train to Coimbra leave?
items.note: o comboio = train in Portugal

items.hr: O comboio com destino ao Porto parte da linha três.
items.en: The train to Porto leaves from platform three.
items.note: linha = platform/track in station announcements

items.hr: O voo para Paris está atrasado quarenta minutos.
items.en: The flight to Paris is delayed forty minutes.
items.note: estar atrasado = to be delayed

items.hr: O embarque começa às nove e um quarto, na porta doze.
items.en: Boarding starts at a quarter past nine, at gate twelve.
items.note: porta de embarque = boarding gate

items.hr: Posso ver o seu cartão de embarque e o passaporte?
items.en: May I see your boarding pass and passport?
items.note: cartão de embarque = boarding pass

items.hr: Onde é a bilheteira? Perdi a ligação para Braga.
items.en: Where is the ticket office? I missed the connection to Braga.
items.note: a bilheteira = ticket office; perder = to miss

items.hr: Pode despachar a mala ou levá-la como bagagem de mão.
items.en: You can check in the suitcase or take it as hand luggage.
items.note: bagagem de mão = hand luggage

activities.title: Horários, linhas e atrasos
activities.intro: Understand announcements and ask the right question at the right counter.

questions.prompt: In European Portuguese, the train is:
questions.explanation: In Portugal the train is 'o comboio'; 'o autocarro' is the bus.

questions.prompt: You hear: 'O comboio parte da linha 5.' What is 'linha' here?
questions.explanation: In station announcements 'linha' means the platform/track the train leaves from.

questions.prompt: 'O voo está atrasado' means the flight is...
questions.explanation: 'Atrasado' = delayed. Cancelled would be 'cancelado'.

questions.prompt: To board a plane you must show your passport and your...
questions.explanation: The boarding pass is 'o cartão de embarque'.

questions.prompt: Ask about the departure time: 'A que horas ___ o próximo comboio para Faro?'
questions.explanation: 'Partir' = to depart: A que horas parte...?

questions.prompt: Type where you buy train tickets at the station: a ___.
questions.explanation: 'A bilheteira' is the ticket office in European Portuguese.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The flight to Madrid is an hour late.'
questions.explanation: Subject + estar + atrasado + duration of the delay.

activities.title: Um atraso no aeroporto
activities.intro: Your flight is delayed. Play 'Me' and get the new details at the desk.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Desculpe, o voo para Londres está atrasado?
lines.en: Excuse me, is the flight to London delayed?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está, sim. Parte agora às seis e meia, com uma hora de atraso.
lines.en: Yes, it is. It now leaves at half past six, one hour late.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E o embarque? Continua na porta quinze?
lines.en: And boarding? Is it still at gate fifteen?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não, mudou para a porta nove. Começa às seis menos um quarto.
lines.en: No, it changed to gate nine. It starts at a quarter to six.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Obrigado. Posso levar esta mala como bagagem de mão?
lines.en: Thank you. Can I take this suitcase as hand luggage?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pode, é pequena. Tenha o cartão de embarque e o passaporte prontos.
lines.en: You can, it's small. Have your boarding pass and passport ready.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está tudo aqui no telemóvel. Obrigado pela ajuda.
lines.en: It's all here on my phone. Thanks for the help.

days.title: De carro
days.objective: Talk about driving in Portugal: carta de condução, portagens on the autoestrada, abastecer o carro, and finding parking.
days.paretoFocus: Five driving nouns (a carta de condução, a portagem, a autoestrada, a gasolineira, o parque de estacionamento) plus 'abastecer' and 'estacionar' cover the road-trip essentials.
drills: Describe a road trip in four sentences: alugar o carro, entrar na autoestrada, pagar a portagem, estacionar.
At the pump, ask for fuel two ways: 'Queria vinte euros de gasóleo' / 'Pode atestar, por favor?'.
Name three places to park: na rua, no parque de estacionamento, num parque subterrâneo.

items: Recall two airport words: porta de embarque, bagagem de mão.
Say the document you need to drive: a carta de condução.
Translate: 'The motorway has tolls.'

activities.title: Conduzir em Portugal
activities.intro: Portuguese motorways ('autoestradas') have tolls ('portagens'), many of them electronic. You drive with a 'carta de condução' and fill up with 'gasolina' or 'gasóleo'.

items.hr: Tirei a carta de condução aos dezoito anos.
items.en: I got my driving licence at eighteen.
items.note: carta de condução = driving licence in Portugal

items.hr: A autoestrada é mais rápida, mas tem portagens.
items.en: The motorway is faster, but it has tolls.
items.note: a portagem = toll

items.hr: Temos de abastecer; o depósito está quase vazio.
items.en: We have to fill up; the tank is almost empty.
items.note: abastecer = to fill up with fuel

items.hr: Este carro é a gasóleo, não é a gasolina.
items.en: This car runs on diesel, not petrol.
items.note: gasóleo = diesel in Portugal

items.hr: Há uma gasolineira na próxima saída.
items.en: There's a petrol station at the next exit.
items.note: a gasolineira / a bomba de gasolina = petrol station

items.hr: É difícil estacionar no centro; procura um parque de estacionamento.
items.en: It's hard to park in the centre; look for a car park.
items.note: estacionar = to park

items.hr: Cuidado, a polícia multa quem conduz depressa demais.
items.en: Careful, the police fine those who drive too fast.
items.note: conduzir = to drive; a multa = fine

activities.title: Na estrada
activities.intro: Driving vocabulary and typical road situations in Portugal.

questions.prompt: The document that lets you drive is:
questions.explanation: In Portugal the driving licence is 'a carta de condução'.

questions.prompt: On the autoestrada you often have to pay...
questions.explanation: Portuguese motorways charge tolls: 'portagens'.

questions.prompt: 'O depósito está quase vazio.' What should you do?
questions.explanation: An almost empty tank means it's time to fill up: abastecer.

questions.prompt: Which fuel is 'gasóleo'?
questions.explanation: In Portugal diesel is 'gasóleo'; 'gasolina' is petrol.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Não há lugares na rua; vou ___ o carro no parque subterrâneo.' (to park)
questions.explanation: 'Estacionar' = to park a vehicle.

questions.prompt: Type the pt-PT word for the fast road with tolls: a ___.
questions.explanation: 'A autoestrada' is the motorway; most Portuguese ones have portagens.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'We have to fill up before getting on the motorway.'
questions.explanation: 'Ter de' + infinitive for obligation; 'antes de' + infinitive for sequence.

activities.title: Viagem de carro para o Sul
activities.intro: You and a friend set off by car. Play 'Me' and handle fuel and tolls.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vamos pela autoestrada ou pela estrada nacional?
lines.en: Shall we take the motorway or the national road?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pela autoestrada. É mais caro por causa das portagens, mas chegamos duas horas mais cedo.
lines.en: The motorway. It's more expensive because of the tolls, but we arrive two hours earlier.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está bem. Olha, o depósito está quase vazio.
lines.en: All right. Look, the tank is almost empty.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então paramos na próxima gasolineira para abastecer. O carro é a gasóleo, certo?
lines.en: Then we stop at the next petrol station to fill up. The car runs on diesel, right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo. Trouxeste a tua carta de condução? Depois conduzes tu.
lines.en: Right. Did you bring your driving licence? You drive later.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trouxe, está na carteira. E em Faro estacionamos no parque ao lado do hotel.
lines.en: I did, it's in my wallet. And in Faro we'll park in the car park next to the hotel.

days.title: Imperativo
days.objective: Give commands and instructions: affirmative 'tu' forms (fala!, come!), formal forms (fale, coma), and negatives (não fales).
days.paretoFocus: One trick generates almost everything: 'tu' affirmative = present 'ele' form (fala, come, abre); formal and ALL negatives use the subjunctive-type form (fale / não fales).
drills: Give five kitchen orders to a friend (tu): corta, mistura, junta, prova, serve.
Repeat the same five orders to a stranger (formal): corte, misture, junte, prove, sirva.
Turn three of them negative for 'tu': não cortes, não mistures, não juntes.

items: Recall three driving words from yesterday: portagem, gasóleo, estacionar.
Say the 'tu' imperative of falar, comer and abrir.
Translate: 'Don't speak so fast!' (to a friend)

activities.title: Dar ordens e instruções
activities.intro: Affirmative 'tu': drop the -s of the present (falas -> fala!). Formal (você/o senhor) and every negative command switch to the opposite-vowel form: fale, coma / não fales, não comas.

items.hr: Fala mais devagar, por favor!
items.en: Speak more slowly, please! (to a friend)
items.note: tu affirmative: falas -> fala

items.hr: Come a sopa antes que arrefeça.
items.en: Eat the soup before it gets cold. (to a friend)
items.note: comes -> come

items.hr: Fale com o médico amanhã, por favor.
items.en: Speak with the doctor tomorrow, please. (formal)
items.note: formal: -ar verbs take -e (fale)

items.hr: Coma devagar e beba muita água.
items.en: Eat slowly and drink lots of water. (formal)
items.note: -er/-ir verbs take -a (coma, beba)

items.hr: Não fales tão alto, o bebé está a dormir.
items.en: Don't talk so loud, the baby is sleeping. (to a friend)
items.note: negative tu = formal form + -s: não fales

items.hr: Não comas isso, já está fora do prazo.
items.en: Don't eat that, it's already past its date. (to a friend)
items.note: não comas, negative always uses the switched vowel

items.hr: Faz os trabalhos de casa! / Vai por ali! / Sê paciente!
items.en: Do your homework! / Go that way! / Be patient! (to a friend)
items.note: irregular tu forms: faz, vai, sê (formal: faça, vá, seja)

activities.title: Ordena, pede, proíbe
activities.intro: Choose or build the right imperative for the person and the polarity.

questions.prompt: To a friend (tu), the affirmative command of 'comer' is:
questions.explanation: Tu affirmative = present 'ele' form: come!

questions.prompt: To a stranger (formal), 'speak with the receptionist' is: '___ com a rececionista.'
questions.explanation: Formal commands of -ar verbs end in -e: fale.

questions.prompt: Make it negative for 'tu': 'Fala com ele.' ->
questions.explanation: Negative tu commands use the switched-vowel form + s: não fales.

questions.prompt: Which is the irregular 'tu' command of 'fazer'?
questions.explanation: 'Fazer' has the short irregular tu imperative: faz! (formal: faça).

questions.prompt: Recipe, formal: '___ a cebola em pedaços pequenos.' (verb: cortar)
questions.explanation: Formal imperative of cortar: corte (-ar -> -e).

questions.prompt: To a friend: '___ a janela, está frio!' (verb: fechar)
questions.explanation: Tu affirmative = present 'ele' form: fecha!

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Don't eat sweets before dinner.'
questions.explanation: Negative command: não + switched form (comas) + object + time phrase.

activities.title: A receita da avó
activities.intro: A friend teaches you a recipe by phone, in commands. Play 'Me' and follow along.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estou pronto. O que faço primeiro?
lines.en: I'm ready. What do I do first?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Primeiro corta a cebola e o alho, mas não cortes os dedos!
lines.en: First chop the onion and the garlic, but don't cut your fingers!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muito engraçado. Já está. E agora?
lines.en: Very funny. Done. And now?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Junta o azeite, mistura bem e deixa cozinhar dez minutos.
lines.en: Add the olive oil, mix well and let it cook for ten minutes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ponho sal agora?
lines.en: Do I add salt now?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não ponhas ainda. Prova primeiro e depois decide.
lines.en: Don't add it yet. Taste it first and then decide.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está ótimo assim. Obrigado, chef!
lines.en: It's great like this. Thanks, chef!

days.title: Direções
days.objective: Give and follow directions with the imperative: vira à direita, segue em frente, atravessa a ponte, plus core city vocabulary.
days.paretoFocus: Four commands (vira, segue, atravessa, para) combined with à direita / à esquerda / em frente let you guide anyone through any Portuguese town.
drills: Guide someone from your home to the nearest café using at least four imperatives.
Say the same route formally: vire, siga, atravesse, pare.
Name five city landmarks with articles: a rotunda, o semáforo, a passadeira, a praça, a paragem de autocarro.

items: Recall the imperative rule: tu affirmative vs formal (vira / vire).
Say the three basic directions: à direita, à esquerda, em frente.
Translate: 'Turn right at the roundabout and go straight on.'

activities.title: Como se vai para...?
activities.intro: Directions in Portugal come as friendly 'tu' commands (vira, segue) or formal ones (vire, siga). Landmarks anchor every instruction: a rotunda, o semáforo, a ponte.

items.hr: Desculpe, como se vai para a estação de comboios?
items.en: Excuse me, how do you get to the train station?
items.note: 'como se vai para...' = how does one get to...

items.hr: Vira à direita no semáforo.
items.en: Turn right at the traffic lights. (to a friend)
items.note: o semáforo = traffic lights

items.hr: Segue em frente até à rotunda.
items.en: Go straight on until the roundabout.
items.note: a rotunda = roundabout

items.hr: Atravessa a ponte e depois vira à esquerda.
items.en: Cross the bridge and then turn left.
items.note: atravessar = to cross

items.hr: Passa pela praça e a igreja fica ao lado do banco.
items.en: Go through the square and the church is next to the bank.
