items.note: ficar ao lado de = to be next to

items.hr: Atravessa na passadeira, é mais seguro.
items.en: Cross at the pedestrian crossing, it's safer.
items.note: a passadeira = zebra crossing in Portugal

items.hr: A paragem de autocarro fica em frente ao museu.
items.en: The bus stop is opposite the museum.
items.note: paragem de autocarro = bus stop in Portugal

activities.title: Guiar pela cidade
activities.intro: Direction commands and city vocabulary in action.

questions.prompt: 'Segue em frente' means:
questions.explanation: 'Em frente' = straight ahead; 'segue em frente' = keep going straight.

questions.prompt: The circular junction where several roads meet is:
questions.explanation: 'A rotunda' is the roundabout, very common in Portugal.

questions.prompt: Say 'turn left' formally, to a stranger:
questions.explanation: Formal imperative of virar: vire (tu would be 'vira').

questions.prompt: Where should a pedestrian cross the street?
questions.explanation: 'A passadeira' is the zebra crossing, the safe place to cross.

questions.prompt: Complete (tu): '___ a ponte e o hotel fica logo à direita.' (verb: atravessar)
questions.explanation: Tu affirmative of atravessar: atravessa!

questions.prompt: Type where you wait for the autocarro: a ___ de autocarro.
questions.explanation: In Portugal the bus stop is 'a paragem de autocarro'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Turn right at the traffic lights and go straight on.'
questions.explanation: Two chained commands: vira à direita + landmark, then segue em frente.

activities.title: Perdido em Lisboa
activities.intro: A tourist asks you the way. Play 'Me' and guide them with imperatives.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Desculpe, sabe como se vai para o castelo?
lines.en: Excuse me, do you know how to get to the castle?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sei, sim. Siga em frente por esta rua até ao semáforo.
lines.en: Yes, I do. Go straight along this street until the traffic lights.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Até ao semáforo, certo. E depois?
lines.en: Until the traffic lights, right. And then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Depois vire à esquerda e atravesse a praça. O castelo fica lá em cima.
lines.en: Then turn left and cross the square. The castle is up there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: É longe? Posso ir de autocarro?
lines.en: Is it far? Can I go by bus?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: São uns quinze minutos a pé. Se preferir, a paragem de autocarro fica ali, em frente à farmácia.
lines.en: It's about fifteen minutes on foot. If you prefer, the bus stop is over there, opposite the pharmacy.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Muito obrigada pela ajuda!
lines.en: Thank you very much for the help!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: De nada. Boa visita!
lines.en: You're welcome. Enjoy your visit!

days.title: Revisão A2 II
days.objective: Consolidate the block: health and doctor visits, pharmacy requests, travel and hotel language, driving words, and the imperative.
days.paretoFocus: Reactivating the five scenario scripts (médico, farmácia, hotel, estação, direções) as whole dialogues fixes them far better than isolated words.
drills: Run the full doctor script solo: marcar a consulta, descrever dois sintomas, perceber a receita.
Give a stranger formal directions from the station to a pharmacy in three commands.
One-minute monologue: describe your last (real or invented) trip, booking, hotel, one problem you complained about.

items: Recall: dói-me vs doem-me; give one example of each.
Recall the imperative pairs: fala/fale, come/coma, vira/vire, and the negative não fales.
Say five pt-PT-only words from this block: pequeno-almoço, casa de banho, autocarro, telemóvel, portagem.

activities.title: Os guiões do bloco
activities.intro: One key sentence per scenario. If you can say all seven from memory, the block is yours.

items.hr: Queria marcar uma consulta porque me dói muito a cabeça.
items.en: I'd like to book an appointment because my head hurts a lot.
items.note: médico: marcar + doer

items.hr: Estou constipado e tenho febre desde ontem.
items.en: I have a cold and a fever since yesterday.
items.note: corpo e queixas

items.hr: Podia dar-me alguma coisa para as dores de garganta?
items.en: Could you give me something for the sore throat?
items.note: farmácia: pedido educado

items.hr: Reservei um quarto duplo com pequeno-almoço incluído.
items.en: I booked a double room with breakfast included.
items.note: viagem + hotel

items.hr: O comboio para o Porto parte da linha dois, com dez minutos de atraso.
items.en: The train to Porto leaves from platform two, ten minutes late.
items.note: estação: linha + atraso

items.hr: Paguei a portagem e depois parei numa gasolineira para abastecer.
items.en: I paid the toll and then stopped at a petrol station to fill up.
items.note: de carro

items.hr: Siga em frente, vire à direita na rotunda e atravesse a passadeira.
items.en: Go straight on, turn right at the roundabout and cross at the zebra crossing.
items.note: direções com imperativo formal

activities.title: Revisão geral
activities.intro: A mixed check across the ten days of this block.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: '___ os pés depois de andar o dia todo.'
questions.explanation: 'Os pés' is plural, so plural doem-me.

questions.prompt: At the pharmacy, the politest request is:
options: Podia dar-me alguma coisa para a febre?
questions.answer: Podia dar-me alguma coisa para a febre?
questions.explanation: 'Podia' + dar-me alguma coisa para... is the polite frame from lesson 3.

questions.prompt: Which set correctly translates 'bus, train, mobile phone, breakfast'?
questions.explanation: Bus, train, mobile and breakfast in Portugal: autocarro, comboio, telemóvel, pequeno-almoço.

questions.prompt: Make it negative for 'tu': 'Vira à esquerda.' ->
questions.explanation: Negative tu commands take the switched form + s: não vires.

questions.prompt: Hotel complaint: 'Desculpe, o chuveiro não ___.'
questions.explanation: The complaint frame from lesson 5: ... não funciona.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Na autoestrada temos de pagar a ___.'
questions.explanation: Portuguese motorways charge 'portagens' (tolls).

questions.prompt: Formal command of 'seguir' for directions: '___ em frente até à praça.'
questions.explanation: Formal imperative of seguir is irregular in the stem: siga.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Take this syrup every eight hours.'
questions.explanation: Formal imperative + object + the dosage interval 'de oito em oito horas'.

activities.title: A consulta completa
activities.intro: The full doctor visit, start to finish. Play 'Me', everything from this block comes together here.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde, sente-se, por favor. Então, o que o traz cá?
lines.en: Good afternoon, sit down, please. So, what brings you here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde, doutora. Não me sinto bem desde sexta-feira. Dói-me a cabeça e doem-me as costas.
lines.en: Good afternoon, doctor. I haven't felt well since Friday. My head hurts and my back hurts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tem febre ou tosse?
lines.en: Do you have a fever or a cough?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tive febre ontem à noite, trinta e oito e meio. E acho que estou constipado: tenho o nariz entupido.
lines.en: I had a fever last night, thirty-eight point five. And I think I have a cold: my nose is blocked.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vou auscultá-lo. Respire fundo... muito bem. Não é nada grave, é uma gripe.
lines.en: I'm going to listen to your chest. Breathe deeply... very good. It's nothing serious, it's the flu.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Que alívio. Preciso de tomar alguma coisa?
lines.en: What a relief. Do I need to take anything?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vou receitar-lhe um medicamento. Tome um comprimido de oito em oito horas, depois das refeições, e beba muita água.
lines.en: I'll prescribe you a medicine. Take one pill every eight hours, after meals, and drink lots of water.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Com esta receita posso comprar tudo na farmácia?
lines.en: With this prescription can I buy everything at the pharmacy?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pode, sim. E descanse: não vá trabalhar antes de quinta-feira.
lines.en: Yes, you can. And rest: don't go to work before Thursday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está combinado. Muito obrigado, doutora, e boa tarde.
lines.en: Agreed. Thank you very much, doctor, and good afternoon.

days.title: Lida da casa
days.objective: Talk about household chores and negotiate how to divide tasks at home.
days.paretoFocus: Six chore verbs (lavar, estender, aspirar, pôr, limpar, arrumar) plus 'É a tua vez de...' cover almost every conversation about housework.
drills: List out loud the six chores you did this week, each with a full sentence in the present.
Negotiate with an imaginary flatmate: 'Eu lavo a loiça se tu estenderes a roupa.'
Describe your Saturday cleaning routine in order, using 'primeiro', 'depois' and 'no fim'.

items: Recall: what do you call the dishes you wash after a meal in Portugal?
Say aloud three chores that use a different verb each: aspirar, estender, pôr.
Translate: 'It's your turn to set the table.'

activities.title: As tarefas domésticas
activities.intro: The core chore vocabulary of a Portuguese home, and how to say whose turn it is.

items.hr: Depois do jantar, eu lavo a loiça.
items.en: After dinner, I wash the dishes.
items.note: 'a loiça', the dishes; in Portugal you 'lavas a loiça', never anything else

items.hr: Vou estender a roupa no estendal da varanda.
items.en: I'm going to hang out the laundry on the balcony drying rack.
items.note: estender a roupa = to hang out the washing

items.hr: Aos sábados aspiro a casa toda.
items.en: On Saturdays I vacuum the whole house.
items.note: aspirar = to vacuum; o aspirador = the vacuum cleaner

items.hr: Podes pôr a mesa? O jantar está quase pronto.
items.en: Can you set the table? Dinner is almost ready.
items.note: pôr a mesa = to set the table; levantar a mesa = to clear it

items.hr: Hoje é a tua vez de passar a ferro.
items.en: Today it's your turn to do the ironing.
items.note: é a tua vez de + infinitive = it's your turn to...

items.hr: Nós dividimos as tarefas: eu limpo a casa de banho e ele arruma a cozinha.
items.en: We split the chores: I clean the bathroom and he tidies the kitchen.
items.note: 'casa de banho' is the European Portuguese word for bathroom

activities.title: Quem faz o quê?
activities.intro: Match each chore to the right verb and negotiate the housework.

questions.prompt: What do you do with 'a loiça' after a meal?
questions.explanation: You 'lavas a loiça' (wash the dishes); estender is for laundry and aspirar for floors.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct chore: 'Antes do jantar, é preciso ___ a mesa.'
questions.explanation: 'Pôr a mesa' means to set the table before the meal.

questions.prompt: Where do you 'estender a roupa'?
questions.explanation: The 'estendal' is the drying rack or clothesline, often on the balcony.

questions.prompt: Complete with the vacuuming verb: 'Ao domingo, gosto de ___ a sala antes do almoço.'
questions.explanation: Aspirar = to vacuum; the machine is 'o aspirador'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Hoje é a tua ___ de lavar a loiça, eu lavei ontem!'
questions.explanation: 'É a tua vez de + infinitive' = it's your turn to do something.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I vacuum the house and you hang out the laundry.'
questions.explanation: A typical chore-splitting sentence: subject + verb for each person, joined by 'e'.

activities.title: Dividir as tarefas
activities.intro: You and your flatmate are organising the weekend cleaning. Play 'Me' and negotiate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A casa está uma confusão. Temos de limpar tudo hoje.
lines.en: The house is a mess. We have to clean everything today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está bem. Eu aspiro a sala e os quartos, pode ser?
lines.en: Alright. I'll vacuum the living room and the bedrooms, okay?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfeito. Então eu limpo a casa de banho e a cozinha.
lines.en: Perfect. Then I'll clean the bathroom and the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E a roupa? A máquina já acabou, alguém tem de a estender.
lines.en: And the laundry? The machine has finished, someone has to hang it out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estendo eu, se tu lavares a loiça do pequeno-almoço.
lines.en: I'll hang it out, if you wash the breakfast dishes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado! E logo à noite pomos a mesa juntos.
lines.en: Deal! And tonight we'll set the table together.

days.title: Avarias e arranjos
days.objective: Report that something at home is broken and arrange for it to be repaired.
days.paretoFocus: 'Avariou', 'não funciona' and 'chamar um técnico' solve nearly every broken-appliance conversation in Portugal.
drills: Report three broken things in your home using 'avariou' or 'não funciona'.
Phone an imaginary technician: say what broke, when, and ask when he can come.
Describe a small disaster: 'A torneira começou a pingar e depois...'

items: Recall: which very Portuguese appliance heats the water for your shower?
Say two ways to report a breakdown: 'avariou' and 'não funciona'.
Translate: 'We need to call a technician.'

activities.title: Está tudo avariado!
activities.intro: How to say things broke down and get them fixed, essential survival Portuguese.

items.hr: A máquina de lavar roupa avariou ontem à noite.
items.en: The washing machine broke down last night.
items.note: avariar = to break down; a avaria = the breakdown

items.hr: O esquentador não funciona e não temos água quente.
items.en: The water heater isn't working and we have no hot water.
items.note: 'o esquentador', the gas water heater, found in most Portuguese homes

items.hr: Temos de chamar um técnico para arranjar o frigorífico.
items.en: We have to call a technician to fix the fridge.
items.note: arranjar = to fix/repair; 'frigorífico' is the pt-PT word for fridge

items.hr: A torneira da cozinha está a pingar há dois dias.
items.en: The kitchen tap has been dripping for two days.
items.note: estar a + infinitive = ongoing action in European Portuguese

items.hr: O lava-loiça está entupido; preciso de um canalizador.
items.en: The sink is blocked; I need a plumber.
items.note: entupido = blocked; o canalizador = the plumber (pt-PT)

items.hr: O elevador está avariado outra vez, temos de subir pelas escadas.
items.en: The lift is out of order again, we have to take the stairs.
items.note: estar avariado = to be out of order

activities.title: O que se avariou?
activities.intro: Report the breakdown and call the right professional.

questions.prompt: You have no hot water for your shower. What probably broke?
questions.explanation: The 'esquentador' heats the water; without it there is no hot shower.

questions.prompt: The sink is 'entupido'. Who do you call?
questions.explanation: A blocked sink is a plumbing problem, so you call 'um canalizador'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'A máquina de lavar ___ esta manhã.'
questions.explanation: A completed past event: the machine broke down this morning, 'avariou'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'O micro-ondas não ___, carrego no botão e não acontece nada.'
questions.explanation: 'Não funciona' = it doesn't work, the standard way to report a fault.

questions.prompt: Complete with the repair verb: 'O técnico vem amanhã ___ o esquentador.'
questions.explanation: In Portugal you say 'arranjar' for repairing appliances.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'We have to call a technician today.'
questions.explanation: 'Ter de + infinitive' expresses obligation: we have to call a technician today.

activities.title: Chamar o técnico
activities.intro: Your water heater died. Play 'Me' and phone the repair service.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Assistência técnica, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.en: Technical support, good afternoon. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde. O meu esquentador avariou e não tenho água quente.
lines.en: Good afternoon. My water heater broke down and I have no hot water.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Compreendo. Desde quando é que não funciona?
lines.en: I understand. Since when has it not been working?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Desde ontem à noite. Faz um barulho estranho e depois desliga-se.
lines.en: Since last night. It makes a strange noise and then switches itself off.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O técnico pode ir aí amanhã de manhã, entre as nove e as onze.
lines.en: The technician can come tomorrow morning, between nine and eleven.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito, fico à espera. Muito obrigado pela ajuda!
lines.en: Perfect, I'll be waiting. Thank you very much for your help!

days.title: Procurar casa
days.objective: Understand rental ads and talk to a landlord about renting a flat in Portugal.
days.paretoFocus: Five words (renda, senhorio, T2, condomínio, anúncio) unlock almost any Portuguese housing conversation or listing.
drills: Read an imaginary ad aloud: 'T2 no centro, renda 800 euros, condomínio incluído.'
Ask three questions to a landlord: about the rent, the deposit and the move-in date.
Describe your dream flat: how many rooms, which floor, with or without varanda.

items: Recall: what does 'T2' mean in a Portuguese property ad?
Say the difference between 'renda' and 'condomínio'.
Translate: 'I would like to visit the flat tomorrow.'

activities.title: À procura de um T2
activities.intro: The vocabulary of Portuguese rental ads and conversations with landlords.

items.hr: Vi um anúncio de um T2 perto do metro.
items.en: I saw an ad for a two-bedroom flat near the metro.
items.note: T2 = flat with two bedrooms; T0 is a studio, T1 has one bedroom

items.hr: A renda é de setecentos euros por mês.
items.en: The rent is seven hundred euros a month.
items.note: a renda = the rent (pt-PT); pagar a renda = to pay the rent

items.hr: O senhorio pede dois meses de caução.
items.en: The landlord asks for two months' deposit.
items.note: o senhorio / a senhoria = landlord / landlady; a caução = deposit

items.hr: O condomínio está incluído na renda?
items.en: Is the building fee included in the rent?
items.note: o condomínio = monthly fee for shared building costs

items.hr: Gostava de visitar o apartamento no sábado de manhã.
items.en: I'd like to visit the flat on Saturday morning.
items.note: 'gostava de', the everyday pt-PT way to say 'I'd like to'

items.hr: A mudança é já na próxima semana; vou alugar uma carrinha.
items.en: The move is next week already; I'm going to rent a van.
items.note: a mudança = the move; fazer a mudança = to move house

activities.title: Ler o anúncio
activities.intro: Decode Portuguese rental ads and ask the landlord the right questions.

questions.prompt: An ad says 'T3 com varanda'. How many bedrooms does the flat have?
questions.explanation: The number after T counts the bedrooms: T3 = three bedrooms.

questions.prompt: Who receives 'a renda' every month?
questions.explanation: The tenant pays the rent to the landlord, 'o senhorio'.

questions.prompt: What is 'o condomínio' in a Portuguese ad?
options: a taxa mensal das despesas do prédio
questions.answer: a taxa mensal das despesas do prédio
questions.explanation: 'Condomínio' is the monthly fee for shared building expenses like the lift and cleaning.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quanto é a ___ por mês? E as despesas estão incluídas?'
questions.explanation: 'A renda' is the monthly rent, the first question to ask about any flat.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'O ___ quer assinar o contrato de arrendamento na sexta-feira.'
questions.explanation: The landlord ('o senhorio') signs the rental contract with the tenant.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I'd like to visit the flat tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: 'Gostava de + infinitive' is the polite pt-PT way to make a request.

activities.title: A visita ao T2
activities.intro: You are visiting a flat with the landlord. Play 'Me' and ask about the details.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bem-vindo! Este é o T2 do anúncio: sala, cozinha e dois quartos.
lines.en: Welcome! This is the two-bedroom flat from the ad: living room, kitchen and two bedrooms.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É muito luminoso! Quanto é a renda por mês?
lines.en: It's very bright! How much is the rent per month?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: São setecentos e cinquenta euros, com o condomínio incluído.
lines.en: It's seven hundred and fifty euros, with the building fee included.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E a caução? Quantos meses é que pede?
lines.en: And the deposit? How many months are you asking for?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dois meses de caução e o primeiro mês de renda adiantado.
lines.en: Two months' deposit and the first month's rent in advance.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parece-me bem. Quando é que posso fazer a mudança?
lines.en: That sounds good to me. When can I move in?

days.title: Comparações
days.objective: Compare flats, cities and everyday things using mais/menos...do que and tão...como.
days.paretoFocus: Three patterns, mais...do que, menos...do que, tão...como, plus irregular melhor/pior handle every A2 comparison.
drills: Compare your current home with your childhood home in three sentences.
Compare two cities you know: size, price, weather, food.
Say three things that are 'tão importantes como' each other in your life.

items: Recall the three comparison patterns: superiority, inferiority, equality.
Say the irregular comparatives of 'bom' and 'mau'.
Translate: 'This flat is bigger than the other one.'

activities.title: Mais, menos, tão... como
activities.intro: How to compare two things in European Portuguese, including the irregular melhor and pior.

items.hr: Este apartamento é mais caro do que o outro.
items.en: This flat is more expensive than the other one.
items.note: mais + adjective + do que = more... than

items.hr: O meu quarto é menos luminoso do que a sala.
items.en: My bedroom is less bright than the living room.
items.note: menos + adjective + do que = less... than

items.hr: O Porto é tão bonito como Lisboa.
items.en: Porto is as beautiful as Lisbon.
items.note: tão + adjective + como = as... as

items.hr: Este café é melhor do que o do supermercado.
items.en: This coffee is better than the supermarket one.
items.note: bom → melhor (never 'mais bom')

items.hr: O trânsito hoje está pior do que ontem.
items.en: The traffic today is worse than yesterday.
items.note: mau → pior (never 'mais mau')

items.hr: A minha rua é mais sossegada, mas o teu bairro tem mais lojas.
items.en: My street is quieter, but your neighbourhood has more shops.
items.note: mais also works with nouns: mais lojas, mais tempo

activities.title: Comparar tudo
activities.intro: Choose or build the right comparison for each situation.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct comparative: 'Este T2 é ___ caro do que o T1.'
questions.explanation: Superiority uses 'mais + adjective + do que': more expensive than.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct comparative of 'bom'?
questions.explanation: 'Bom' has the irregular comparative 'melhor'; 'mais bom' is wrong.

questions.prompt: Complete the equality: 'O comboio é ___ rápido ___ o autocarro.'
questions.explanation: Equality uses 'tão + adjective + como': as fast as.

questions.prompt: Complete with the irregular comparative of 'mau': 'O tempo hoje está ___ do que ontem, não para de chover.'
questions.explanation: 'Mau' becomes 'pior' in comparisons: worse than.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A renda no centro é mais alta ___ que na periferia.'
questions.explanation: The full comparison connector is 'do que': mais alta do que.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Lisbon is bigger than Porto.'
questions.explanation: 'Grande' has the irregular comparative 'maior': bigger than.

activities.title: Qual dos dois apartamentos?
activities.intro: You visited two flats and now compare them with a friend. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, já visitaste os dois apartamentos? Qual preferes?
lines.en: So, have you visited both flats? Which do you prefer?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O do centro é mais bonito, mas é mais caro do que o outro.
lines.en: The one in the centre is prettier, but it's more expensive than the other one.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o segundo? É tão luminoso como o primeiro?
lines.en: And the second one? Is it as bright as the first?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, é menos luminoso, mas tem uma cozinha melhor.
lines.en: No, it's less bright, but it has a better kitchen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o bairro? Isso é tão importante como a casa.
lines.en: And the neighbourhood? That's as important as the house.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens razão. O bairro do segundo é mais sossegado, acho que fico com esse.
lines.en: You're right. The second one's neighbourhood is quieter, I think I'll take that one.

days.title: Superlativos
days.objective: Use 'o mais...' and '-íssimo' to talk about favourite things and the best of everything.
days.paretoFocus: 'O/a mais + adjective' and the ending '-íssimo' let you praise (or exaggerate) anything, the heart of Portuguese banter.
drills: Name the best restaurant, the cheapest café and the most beautiful street in your area.
Exaggerate five adjectives with -íssimo: caríssimo, lindíssima, dificílimo...
Defend your city against a rival city in three superlative sentences.

items: Recall: how do you form 'the most expensive' in Portuguese?
Say the irregular superlatives: o melhor, o pior, o maior.
Translate: 'This is the best beach in the country.'

activities.title: O melhor de tudo
activities.intro: Relative superlatives with 'o mais...' and absolute superlatives with '-íssimo'.

items.hr: Este é o café mais famoso da cidade.
items.en: This is the most famous café in the city.
items.note: o/a + noun + mais + adjective + de = the most... in

items.hr: A Sofia é a pessoa mais simpática que conheço.
items.en: Sofia is the nicest person I know.
items.note: superlative + 'que conheço' = ...that I know

items.hr: Este restaurante é caríssimo, mas a comida é ótima.
items.en: This restaurant is really expensive, but the food is great.
items.note: caro → caríssimo; -íssimo intensifies without comparing

items.hr: O exame foi dificílimo, mas correu bem.
items.en: The exam was extremely hard, but it went well.
items.note: difícil → dificílimo (irregular -íssimo form)

items.hr: Para mim, o melhor pastel de nata é o de Belém.
items.en: For me, the best custard tart is the one from Belém.
items.note: bom → o melhor; mau → o pior

items.hr: Lisboa é a maior cidade do país, mas o Porto tem a ponte mais bonita.
items.en: Lisbon is the biggest city in the country, but Porto has the most beautiful bridge.
items.note: grande → o maior; classic Lisboa vs Porto banter

activities.title: Só superlativos
activities.intro: Build superlatives, regular and irregular, and exaggerate like a local.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct superlative: 'Esta é a praia ___ bonita do Algarve.'
questions.explanation: The relative superlative is 'a mais + adjective + de': the most beautiful in the Algarve.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct -íssimo form of 'caro'?
questions.explanation: Drop the final vowel and add -íssimo: caro → caríssimo.

questions.prompt: Choose the irregular superlative: 'Este é ___ restaurante da cidade, a comida é péssima!'
questions.explanation: 'Mau' uses the irregular superlative 'o pior'; 'o mais mau' is wrong.

questions.prompt: Complete with the irregular superlative of 'bom': 'Para mim, este é o ___ gelado de Lisboa.'
questions.explanation: Bom → o melhor: the best ice cream in Lisbon.

questions.prompt: Complete with the -íssimo form of 'lindo': 'A vista do miradouro é ___!'
questions.explanation: Lindo → lindíssimo; it agrees with 'a vista', so lindíssima.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Porto has the most beautiful bridge in the country.'
questions.explanation: Relative superlative: a ponte mais bonita do país = the most beautiful bridge in the country.

activities.title: Lisboa ou Porto?
activities.intro: A friend from Porto teases you about Lisbon. Play 'Me' and answer with superlatives.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tenho de dizer: o Porto é a cidade mais bonita de Portugal.
lines.en: I have to say it: Porto is the most beautiful city in Portugal.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Calma! Lisboa tem os miradouros mais famosos e a luz é lindíssima.
lines.en: Easy there! Lisbon has the most famous viewpoints and the light is stunning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pode ser, mas a francesinha é o melhor prato do mundo.
lines.en: Maybe, but the francesinha is the best dish in the world.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O melhor do mundo? O pastel de nata é muito mais famoso!
lines.en: The best in the world? The custard tart is much more famous!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está bem, mas admite: o café em Lisboa é caríssimo.
lines.en: Fine, but admit it: coffee in Lisbon is super expensive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É verdade... mas continua a ser a maior cidade e a minha preferida!
lines.en: That's true... but it's still the biggest city and my favourite!

days.title: Desporto e ginásio
days.objective: Talk about sports you practise, gym routines and weekend matches.
days.paretoFocus: 'Praticar desporto', 'ir ao ginásio' and 'jogar + game' cover nearly every fitness conversation, and in Portugal it's always 'desporto', never anything else.
drills: Say which sports you practise, how often, and where.
Describe your ideal gym session: warm-up, exercises, stretching.
Tell someone about the last match you watched: who played, who won, the score.

items: Recall: the pt-PT word for sport and the pt-PT word for a football goal.
Say three sports with 'jogar' and two activities with 'fazer'.
Translate: 'Our team won the match two-nil.'

activities.title: Mexer o corpo
activities.intro: Sports vocabulary the European way: desporto, equipa, golo, ginásio.

items.hr: Pratico desporto três vezes por semana.
items.en: I do sport three times a week.
items.note: 'desporto' is the European Portuguese word for sport

items.hr: Vou ao ginásio antes do trabalho, às sete da manhã.
items.en: I go to the gym before work, at seven in the morning.
items.note: ir ao ginásio = to go to the gym

items.hr: Jogo futebol com os colegas ao sábado.
items.en: I play football with my colleagues on Saturdays.
items.note: jogar + game: jogar futebol, ténis, basquetebol

items.hr: A nossa equipa ganhou o jogo por dois a um.
items.en: Our team won the match two-one.
items.note: 'a equipa' (pt-PT) = the team; ganhar/perder o jogo

items.hr: O avançado marcou um golo fantástico no último minuto.
items.en: The striker scored a fantastic goal in the last minute.
items.note: 'o golo' is the pt-PT word for a goal in football

items.hr: Depois de treinar, faço sempre alongamentos.
items.en: After training, I always do stretches.
items.note: treinar = to train; fazer alongamentos = to stretch

activities.title: No ginásio e no campo
activities.intro: Pick the right sports verb and the right European Portuguese word.

questions.prompt: Which is the European Portuguese word for 'sport'?
questions.explanation: In Portugal the word is 'desporto', 'praticar desporto' = to do sport.

questions.prompt: In Portugal, what did the player score when the crowd shouted?
questions.explanation: 'Marcar um golo' = to score a goal, the pt-PT football word.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct verb: 'Ao domingo, eu ___ ténis com a minha irmã.'
questions.explanation: Games with a ball or opponent use 'jogar': jogo ténis.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Vou ao ___ três vezes por semana para treinar.'
questions.explanation: 'O ginásio' is where you train: ir ao ginásio.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pt-PT word for team: 'A minha ___ ganhou o campeonato este ano!'
questions.explanation: In Portugal a team is 'a equipa'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'We train at the gym twice a week.'
questions.explanation: Frequency goes at the end: duas vezes por semana = twice a week.

activities.title: O jogo do fim de semana
activities.intro: Monday morning: your colleague wants to know about your weekend match. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como correu o jogo de sábado?
lines.en: So, how did Saturday's match go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Correu muito bem! A nossa equipa ganhou por três a dois.
lines.en: It went really well! Our team won three-two.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa! E tu, marcaste algum golo?
lines.en: Nice! And you, did you score any goals?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Marquei um de cabeça no segundo tempo. Foi fantástico!
lines.en: I scored a header in the second half. It was fantastic!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tenho de começar a praticar desporto outra vez. Posso ir treinar convosco?
lines.en: I have to start doing sport again. Can I come train with you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Claro! Treinamos no campo perto do ginásio, às quartas à noite.
lines.en: Of course! We train at the pitch near the gym, on Wednesday evenings.

days.title: Um dia na praia
days.objective: Plan and describe a day at the beach, from packing the bag to eating an ice cream.
days.paretoFocus: Beach life is central to Portuguese summers: toalha, protetor solar, ondas and 'apanhar sol' carry the whole conversation.
drills: List everything you put in your beach bag, out loud, in Portuguese.
Describe your perfect beach day from morning to sunset.
Warn a friend about the sun and the waves in two sentences.

items: Recall: what must you put on before 'apanhar sol'?
Say three things you bring to the beach and one thing you buy there.
Translate: 'The waves are too big today.'

activities.title: Rumo à praia
activities.intro: Everything you need for a Portuguese beach day, from the towel to the ice cream.

items.hr: Não te esqueças da toalha e do chapéu de sol.
items.en: Don't forget the towel and the beach umbrella.
items.note: o chapéu de sol = beach umbrella (pt-PT)

items.hr: Põe protetor solar antes de apanhar sol.
items.en: Put on sunscreen before sunbathing.
items.note: apanhar sol = to sunbathe; apanhar um escaldão = to get sunburnt

items.hr: As ondas hoje estão perfeitas para fazer surf.
items.en: The waves today are perfect for surfing.
items.note: as ondas = the waves; o mar está calmo/agitado

items.hr: Vamos dar um mergulho antes do almoço?
items.en: Shall we go for a dip before lunch?
items.note: dar um mergulho = to take a dip

items.hr: As crianças estão a fazer castelos de areia à beira-mar.
items.en: The children are making sandcastles by the sea.
items.note: a areia = sand; à beira-mar = by the seaside

items.hr: Está imenso calor, vou comprar um gelado ao bar da praia.
items.en: It's really hot, I'm going to buy an ice cream at the beach bar.
items.note: 'o gelado' is the pt-PT word for ice cream

activities.title: A mala da praia
activities.intro: Pack the right things and use the beach expressions correctly.

questions.prompt: What do you put on your skin to avoid 'um escaldão'?
questions.explanation: Sunscreen ('protetor solar') protects you from sunburn ('escaldão').

questions.prompt: What does 'apanhar sol' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Apanhar sol' literally 'to catch sun' = to sunbathe.

questions.prompt: The sea is dangerous today. What do you say?
questions.explanation: Big waves ('ondas grandes') are the warning sign of a dangerous sea.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Estendi a ___ na areia e deitei-me ao sol.'
questions.explanation: You spread your towel ('a toalha') on the sand before sunbathing.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Está tanto calor! Apetece-me um ___ de morango.'
questions.explanation: 'O gelado' is the European Portuguese word for ice cream.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Let's go for a dip in the sea now.'
questions.explanation: 'Dar um mergulho' = to take a dip; 'vamos + infinitive' makes the suggestion.

activities.title: Manhã na Costa da Caparica
activities.intro: You arrive at the beach with a friend. Play 'Me' and set up your spot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que dia perfeito! Onde é que pomos as toalhas?
lines.en: What a perfect day! Where shall we put the towels?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ali perto das rochas, à sombra do chapéu de sol.
lines.en: Over there near the rocks, in the shade of the beach umbrella.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa ideia. Já puseste protetor solar?
lines.en: Good idea. Have you already put on sunscreen?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda não. Põe-me nas costas, por favor, não quero apanhar um escaldão.
lines.en: Not yet. Put some on my back, please, I don't want to get sunburnt.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pronto! As ondas estão calmas, vamos dar um mergulho?
lines.en: Done! The waves are calm, shall we go for a dip?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vamos! E depois compramos gelados no bar da praia.
lines.en: Let's go! And afterwards we'll buy ice creams at the beach bar.

days.title: Festas e tradições
days.objective: Talk about Portuguese festivals, birthdays, presents and how to invite people.
days.paretoFocus: Santos populares vocabulary plus 'convidar', 'prenda' and 'Parabéns!' handle every celebration from June sardines to birthday parties.
drills: Invite a friend to a party: say when, where, and what to bring.
Describe the last birthday you celebrated: the cake, the presents, the guests.
Explain the santos populares to a foreigner in three sentences.

items: Recall: what do people eat in the streets during the santos populares?
Say the pt-PT word for a present and the birthday greeting.
Translate: 'I'd like to invite you to my birthday party.'

activities.title: Há festa na rua
activities.intro: From June street parties to birthdays: celebrating the Portuguese way.

items.hr: Em junho, os santos populares enchem as ruas de Lisboa e do Porto.
items.en: In June, the popular saints' festivals fill the streets of Lisbon and Porto.
items.note: Santo António (Lisboa, 13/6) and São João (Porto, 24/6)

items.hr: Comemos sardinhas assadas e bebemos sangria na rua.
items.en: We eat grilled sardines and drink sangria in the street.
items.note: sardinha assada = grilled sardine, the taste of June

items.hr: As marchas populares desfilam pela Avenida da Liberdade.
items.en: The popular parades march down Avenida da Liberdade.
items.note: as marchas = neighbourhood parades with music and costumes

items.hr: No sábado faço anos, vou fazer uma festa em casa.
items.en: On Saturday it's my birthday, I'm going to throw a party at home.
items.note: fazer anos = to have a birthday (pt-PT)

items.hr: Ainda não comprei a prenda para a Rita.
items.en: I still haven't bought Rita's present.
items.note: 'a prenda' is the everyday pt-PT word for a gift

items.hr: Quero convidar-te para o meu aniversário. Parabéns cantam-se à meia-noite!
items.en: I want to invite you to my birthday. Happy birthday is sung at midnight!
items.note: convidar alguém para = to invite someone to; 'Parabéns!' = happy birthday

activities.title: Vamos festejar
activities.intro: Festivals, birthdays and invitations, choose and complete.

questions.prompt: What do people traditionally eat in the streets during the santos populares?
questions.explanation: Grilled sardines are the iconic street food of the June festivals.

questions.prompt: What are 'as marchas' in Lisbon in June?
options: desfiles populares com música e trajes
questions.answer: desfiles populares com música e trajes
questions.explanation: The 'marchas populares' are neighbourhood parades with music and costumes.

questions.prompt: Your friend says 'Faço anos amanhã.' What does it mean?
options: They start a new job tomorrow.
They are turning in an essay tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 'Fazer anos' is the pt-PT expression for having a birthday.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pt-PT word for gift: 'Comprei uma ___ para o aniversário do Pedro.'
questions.explanation: 'A prenda' is the everyday European Portuguese word for a present.

questions.prompt: Complete the invitation verb: 'Quero ___ os meus colegas para a festa de sábado.'
questions.explanation: 'Convidar alguém para' = to invite someone to an event.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'At the São João festival we eat sardines in the street.'
questions.explanation: Time expression first, then the shared June tradition: eating sardines in the street.

activities.title: Convite para a festa
activities.intro: A friend invites you to the Santo António festivities. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Olha, na sexta é Santo António. Vens connosco a Alfama?
lines.en: Hey, Friday is Santo António. Are you coming with us to Alfama?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Claro que sim! Nunca vi as marchas ao vivo.
lines.en: Of course! I've never seen the parades live.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vais adorar. Há música, manjericos e sardinhas assadas em todo o lado.
lines.en: You'll love it. There's music, basil plants and grilled sardines everywhere.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Que bom! E no sábado é o aniversário da Ana, não é?
lines.en: How nice! And Saturday is Ana's birthday, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: É! Ela convidou toda a gente. Já compraste a prenda?
lines.en: It is! She invited everyone. Have you bought the present yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda não, mas amanhã trato disso. Vai ser um fim de semana de festa!
lines.en: Not yet, but I'll take care of it tomorrow. It's going to be a weekend of partying!

days.title: Estar a + infinitivo: a ação em curso
days.objective: You can say what is happening right now, and what was happening at a past moment, using estar a plus the infinitive.
days.paretoFocus: European Portuguese does not use the -ndo gerund of Brazilian Portuguese, so estar a plus infinitive is the only natural way to talk about an action in progress.
drills: Look around you and say five sentences about what people near you are doing right now.
Take five things you did yesterday evening and turn each one into 'ontem, as oito, eu estava a ...'.
Answer out loud, three different ways, the question 'O que estas a fazer?'

items: Which verb do you conjugate in estar a plus infinitive, and which one stays unchanged?
How do you turn 'estou a comer' into a past ongoing action?
Say in Portuguese: it is raining again.

activities.title: O que estas a fazer?
activities.intro: To describe an action in progress, Portugal uses estar plus the preposition a plus the infinitive. Only estar changes; the second verb always stays in the infinitive. Put estar in the imperfect to describe what was going on at a moment in the past.

items.hr: Não posso falar agora, estou a jantar.
items.en: I can't talk now, I'm having dinner.
items.note: estar in the present + a + infinitive

items.hr: O que estás a fazer no quarto?
items.en: What are you doing in the bedroom?
items.note: tu form: estás a

items.hr: A Rita está a trabalhar num projeto novo.
items.en: Rita is working on a new project.
items.note: fazer, trabalhar, comer: the infinitive never changes

items.hr: Estávamos a ver televisão quando o telefone tocou.
items.en: We were watching television when the phone rang.
items.note: imperfect of estar for a past action in progress

items.hr: Está a chover outra vez.
items.en: It's raining again.
items.note: weather in progress: está a chover, está a nevar

activities.title: Neste momento
activities.intro: Practise describing actions in progress in the present and in the past.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Silêncio! O bebé ___ dormir.'
questions.explanation: The structure is estar conjugated plus a plus the infinitive: está a dormir.

questions.prompt: Which sentence describes something happening at this very moment?
options: Leio o jornal todos os dias.
Estou a ler o jornal neste momento.
Li o jornal ontem de manhã.
questions.answer: Estou a ler o jornal neste momento.
questions.explanation: Only estar a plus infinitive marks an action in progress; the others are habit, past and future.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of estar: 'Eles ___ a estudar para o teste.'
questions.explanation: Third person plural of estar in the present is estão.

questions.prompt: Complete so the sentence describes what you were busy with at eight last night: 'Ontem, às oito, eu ___ a preparar o jantar.'
questions.explanation: A past action in progress takes the imperfect of estar: eu estava a preparar.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'She is talking on the phone with her mother.'
questions.explanation: Estar comes right after the subject and the infinitive follows the preposition a.

activities.title: Um telefonema a meio do jantar
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend calls at a bad moment and you explain what everyone at home is doing right now.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estou? Olá, Rita. Estás ocupada?
lines.en: Hello? Hi, Rita. Are you busy?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Um bocadinho. Estou a jantar com a minha família.
lines.en: A little. I'm having dinner with my family.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Desculpa. E o teu irmão, também está aí?
lines.en: Sorry. And your brother, is he there too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está, mas está a estudar no quarto para um teste.
lines.en: He is, but he's studying in his room for a test.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Telefonei há uma hora e ninguém atendeu.
lines.en: I called an hour ago and nobody answered.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois, estávamos a passear o cão nessa altura.
lines.en: Right, we were walking the dog at that time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então telefono depois. Boa noite!
lines.en: I'll call later then. Good night!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado. Estou a acabar de comer e já te ligo.
lines.en: Agreed. I'm finishing eating and I'll call you right back.

days.title: Advérbios em -mente
days.objective: You can build and use adverbs of manner in -mente to say how something is done.
days.paretoFocus: One simple rule turns hundreds of adjectives you already know into adverbs, which instantly makes your sentences sound more precise and more adult.
drills: Take ten adjectives you already know and write the -mente adverb for each one.
Write three sentences about your own habits using normalmente, geralmente and raramente.
Say one sentence with two adverbs joined by e, dropping -mente from the first.

items: Which form of the adjective do you start from when you build an adverb in -mente?
What happens to a written accent when you add -mente?
Say in Portuguese: unfortunately, the train is late.

activities.title: Como é que se faz?
activities.intro: Adverbs of manner are built from the feminine singular of the adjective plus -mente: rápida becomes rapidamente. Adjectives that do not change for gender simply add -mente: feliz becomes felizmente. Any written accent on the adjective disappears, because the stress moves to -mente.

items.hr: Ela fala rapidamente, mas percebe-se tudo.
items.en: She speaks quickly, but you can understand everything.
items.note: rápida + mente, and the accent drops

items.hr: Infelizmente, o comboio está atrasado.
items.en: Unfortunately, the train is late.
items.note: infeliz has one form for both genders, so just add -mente

items.hr: Ele leu o contrato cuidadosamente.
items.en: He read the contract carefully.
items.note: cuidadosa + mente

items.hr: Provavelmente vou chegar depois das oito.
items.en: I'll probably arrive after eight.
items.note: adverbs of probability often open the sentence

items.hr: O senhor conduz lenta e cuidadosamente.
items.en: The gentleman drives slowly and carefully.
items.note: in a list, only the last adverb keeps -mente

activities.title: De -mente em -mente
activities.intro: Practise forming adverbs from adjectives and placing them in the sentence.

questions.prompt: Which is the adverb formed from the adjective 'fácil'?
questions.explanation: Fácil has one form for both genders, so you just add -mente, and the accent is lost.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ele respondeu ___ a todas as perguntas do professor.'
questions.explanation: A verb is modified by an adverb, not by an adjective: correta plus -mente.

questions.prompt: Turn the adjective 'lento' into an adverb: 'O autocarro anda muito ___ nesta rua.'
questions.explanation: Start from the feminine lenta and add -mente.

questions.prompt: Build an adverb from 'normal' to open the sentence: '___, apanho o comboio das oito.'
questions.explanation: Normal has a single form for both genders, so the adverb is normal plus -mente.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Unfortunately, the shop is already closed.'
questions.explanation: Sentence adverbs in -mente usually come first, followed by a comma.

activities.title: Como correu a apresentação?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks how a presentation went and you answer using adverbs in -mente.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como correu a apresentação?
lines.en: So, how did the presentation go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Correu surpreendentemente bem, obrigado.
lines.en: It went surprisingly well, thank you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Falaste muito depressa, como da última vez?
lines.en: Did you speak very fast, like last time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, desta vez falei lenta e claramente.
lines.en: No, this time I spoke slowly and clearly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o diretor disse alguma coisa?
lines.en: And did the director say anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Disse que preparei tudo cuidadosamente.
lines.en: He said that I prepared everything carefully.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ainda bem. E o projeto avança?
lines.en: Good. And is the project moving forward?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Provavelmente sim, mas infelizmente só no outono.
lines.en: Probably yes, but unfortunately only in the autumn.

days.title: A negação: nunca, ninguém, nada, nenhum
days.objective: You can deny and refuse precisely using nunca, ninguém, nada and nenhum, including the double negative that Portuguese requires.
days.paretoFocus: Portuguese keeps não in front of the verb even when a second negative word follows, and getting this wrong is one of the clearest signs of a foreign speaker.
drills: Answer five yes or no questions about yourself, all in the negative, using a different negative word each time.
Take three positive sentences you wrote earlier this week and make each one negative.
Say the same idea twice: once with the negative word before the verb, once after it with não.

items: When can you drop não from a negative sentence?
Which negative word agrees in gender and number with the noun?
Say in Portuguese: I don't know anyone here.

activities.title: Nunca, ninguém, nada
activities.intro: Portuguese uses double negatives: if the negative word comes after the verb, não must stay in front of the verb. If the negative word comes before the verb, não disappears. Nenhum is the only one of these words that agrees with the noun.

items.hr: Nunca fui a Braga.
items.en: I have never been to Braga.
items.note: the negative word is before the verb, so no não

items.hr: Não conheço ninguém nesta cidade.
items.en: I don't know anyone in this city.
items.note: negative word after the verb, so não is obligatory

items.hr: Não quero nada, obrigado.
items.en: I don't want anything, thank you.
items.note: nada is for things, ninguém for people

items.hr: Não há nenhum comboio depois da meia-noite.
items.en: There is no train after midnight.
items.note: nenhum, nenhuma, nenhuns, nenhumas agree with the noun

items.hr: Ninguém me disse nada sobre a reunião.
items.en: Nobody told me anything about the meeting.
items.note: two negative words together are perfectly normal

activities.title: Dizer que não
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right negative word and placing não correctly.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Não vi ___ na festa; nem o Pedro nem a Ana lá estavam.'
questions.explanation: Ninguém refers to people; nada refers to things.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct European Portuguese?
questions.explanation: Não stays before the verb and nenhum agrees with the masculine singular noun problema.

questions.prompt: Complete with the negative word for things: 'Não comi ___ ao pequeno-almoço, só bebi um café.'
questions.explanation: Nada is the negative pronoun for things, and não stays in front of the verb.

questions.prompt: Complete so the sentence says he is always late and is never on time: 'Ele ___ chega a horas.'
questions.explanation: Because the negative adverb comes before the verb, não is not used.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'There is no pharmacy open near here.'
questions.explanation: Nenhuma is feminine to agree with farmácia, and não is kept because the negative word follows the verb.

activities.title: O primeiro dia no emprego novo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. It is your first day at a new workplace and you answer a colleague's questions in the negative.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já conheces alguém aqui na equipa?
lines.en: Do you already know anyone here in the team?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não conheço ninguém, é o meu primeiro dia.
lines.en: I don't know anyone, it's my first day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já trabalhaste num escritório assim tão grande?
lines.en: Have you ever worked in such a big office?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nunca trabalhei num sítio com tanta gente.
lines.en: I have never worked in a place with so many people.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Precisas de alguma coisa para começar?
lines.en: Do you need anything to get started?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não preciso de nada, já tenho o computador.
lines.en: I don't need anything, I already have the computer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tens alguma dúvida sobre o horário?
lines.en: Do you have any question about the schedule?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não tenho nenhuma dúvida, o chefe explicou tudo.
lines.en: I don't have any question, the boss explained everything.

days.title: Já, ainda e ainda não
days.objective: You can say whether something has happened already, has not happened yet, is still going on, or is no longer the case.
days.paretoFocus: These four little combinations carry most of the everyday information about progress and change, and they appear in almost every conversation about plans and errands.
drills: Make a list of five errands and mark each one as já feito or ainda não feito, out loud.
Describe three things about your life that ainda são verdade and two that já não são.
Ask three questions with já and answer each one both ways.

items: What is the difference between ainda não and já não?
Which word means still, and which means already?
Say in Portuguese: I haven't finished the report yet.

activities.title: Já e ainda
activities.intro: Já means already, and in a question it means yet. Ainda means still, and ainda não means not yet. Já não means not any more, and marks a change from a previous situation.

items.hr: Já almoçaste? Já, obrigado.
items.en: Have you had lunch yet? Yes, already, thanks.
items.note: já alone is a full answer

items.hr: Ainda não acabei o relatório.
items.en: I haven't finished the report yet.
items.note: ainda não = not yet

items.hr: Ela ainda mora com os pais.
items.en: She still lives with her parents.
items.note: ainda = still, the situation continues

items.hr: Já não trabalho naquele banco.
items.en: I don't work at that bank any more.
items.note: já não marks a change

items.hr: O médico ainda não chegou, mas a enfermeira já está aqui.
items.en: The doctor hasn't arrived yet, but the nurse is already here.
items.note: the two contrast naturally in one sentence

activities.title: Já ou ainda?
activities.intro: Practise choosing between já, já não, ainda and ainda não.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ele mudou de emprego, ___ trabalha no banco.'
questions.explanation: The job changed, so the old situation has ended: já não means not any more.

questions.prompt: What does 'Ainda não recebi a encomenda' mean?
options: I have already received the parcel.
I have not received the parcel yet.
questions.answer: I have not received the parcel yet.
questions.explanation: Ainda não expresses something expected that has not happened so far.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word so the sentence means she is still asleep: 'Ela ___ está a dormir.'
questions.explanation: Ainda shows that the situation started earlier and continues.

questions.prompt: Complete so the sentence explains that buying bread is unnecessary: 'Não precisas de comprar pão, eu ___ comprei.'
questions.explanation: Já places the action before the moment of speaking.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I have not spoken to the landlord yet.'
questions.explanation: Ainda comes before não, and both come before the verb.

activities.title: Ainda falta muito?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your flatmate checks what is done and what is missing before guests arrive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Os teus pais chegam às oito. Já arrumaste a sala?
lines.en: Your parents arrive at eight. Have you tidied the living room yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Já arrumei tudo, só falta pôr a mesa.
lines.en: I've already tidied everything, only setting the table is left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E a sobremesa, já está pronta?
lines.en: And the dessert, is it ready yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda não está, ainda está no forno.
lines.en: It isn't ready yet, it's still in the oven.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Precisamos de comprar vinho?
lines.en: Do we need to buy wine?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Já não é preciso, trouxe duas garrafas ontem.
lines.en: It's not necessary any more, I brought two bottles yesterday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ótimo. Então ainda temos tempo para um café.
lines.en: Great. Then we still have time for a coffee.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Temos, mas ainda não lavei a loiça toda.
lines.en: We do, but I haven't washed all the dishes yet.

days.title: Desde, há e durante: situar no tempo
days.objective: You can say since when, for how long, and during which period something happened.
days.paretoFocus: Almost every conversation about your life, job or studies needs one of these three words, and each one answers a different question that learners routinely confuse.
drills: Say three true sentences about yourself with desde and three with há.
Describe a finished period of your life in one sentence with durante.
Ask a friend three questions beginning with Há quanto tempo.

items: Which of the three words is followed by a starting point rather than a length of time?
Which one is used for a period that is completely finished?
Say in Portuguese: I have lived here for three years.

activities.title: Desde quando?
activities.intro: Desde introduces the starting point of a situation that is still true: desde 2019, desde segunda-feira. Há introduces how long that situation has lasted: há três anos. Durante introduces a period seen as complete, usually with a past tense.

items.hr: Moro em Lisboa desde 2019.
items.en: I have lived in Lisbon since 2019.
items.note: desde + a point in time

items.hr: Vivo neste bairro há três anos.
items.en: I have lived in this neighbourhood for three years.
items.note: há + a length of time, present tense in Portuguese

items.hr: Estudei português durante seis meses.
items.en: I studied Portuguese for six months.
items.note: durante + a closed period, past tense

items.hr: Desde que mudei de emprego, durmo melhor.
items.en: Since I changed jobs, I sleep better.
items.note: desde que introduces a whole clause

items.hr: Não o vejo há muito tempo.
items.en: I haven't seen him for a long time.
items.note: há muito tempo, há pouco tempo

activities.title: Quanto tempo?
activities.intro: Practise choosing between desde, há and durante.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Trabalho nesta empresa ___ cinco anos e continuo cá.'
questions.explanation: Cinco anos is a length of time for a situation that continues, so the word is há.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Estive em Espanha ___ duas semanas e voltei no domingo.'
questions.explanation: The stay is over and its whole length is being described, which calls for durante.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word that introduces a starting point: 'Ela conhece o Pedro ___ o liceu.'
questions.explanation: O liceu is the moment the friendship started, not its length.

questions.prompt: Complete the question so it asks how long someone has been waiting: '___ quanto tempo estás à espera?'
questions.explanation: Há quanto tempo is the fixed question for duration up to now.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I have not been to the dentist for two years.'
questions.explanation: Portuguese uses the present tense with há for a situation that still holds.

activities.title: Um reencontro no café
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You meet an old acquaintance and you both place events in time with desde, há and durante.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não te via há imenso tempo! Ainda vives no Porto?
lines.en: I hadn't seen you for ages! Do you still live in Porto?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, moro em Coimbra desde o ano passado.
lines.en: No, I've lived in Coimbra since last year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E gostas? Há quanto tempo estás nessa empresa?
lines.en: And do you like it? How long have you been at that company?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estou lá há oito meses e gosto muito.
lines.en: I've been there for eight months and I like it a lot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Antes disso estiveste no estrangeiro, não foi?
lines.en: Before that you were abroad, weren't you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estive, trabalhei em Bruxelas durante dois anos.
lines.en: I was, I worked in Brussels for two years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E desde que voltaste, sentes-te melhor?
lines.en: And since you came back, do you feel better?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muito melhor, mas não vejo os meus amigos de lá há meses.
lines.en: Much better, but I haven't seen my friends from there for months.

days.title: Os indefinidos: alguém, algo, cada, vários
days.objective: You can refer to unspecified people, things and quantities with alguém, alguma coisa, algum, cada and vários.
days.paretoFocus: Indefinites let you talk about people and things whose identity you do not know or do not need to state, which is essential for questions, offers and vague plans.
drills: Ask five questions using alguém or alguma coisa.
Describe your week using cada with three different singular nouns.
Write three sentences with vários and várias, checking the agreement each time.

items: Which indefinite never changes form and is always followed by a singular noun?
Which indefinite refers to an unnamed person?
Say in Portuguese: someone is waiting for you at reception.

activities.title: Alguém, cada, vários
activities.intro: Alguém stands for an unnamed person and never changes. For things, spoken Portuguese prefers alguma coisa to algo. Cada is invariable and always takes a singular noun, while algum and vário agree in gender and number with the noun.

items.hr: Alguém bateu à porta.
items.en: Someone knocked at the door.
items.note: alguém is always singular and invariable

items.hr: Queres beber alguma coisa?
items.en: Do you want something to drink?
items.note: alguma coisa is far more common in speech than algo

items.hr: Cada aluno tem um cacifo.
items.en: Each student has a locker.
items.note: cada + singular noun, never cada alunos

items.hr: Comprei vários livros na feira.
items.en: I bought several books at the fair.
items.note: vários, várias agree with the noun

items.hr: Alguns colegas ficaram até tarde.
items.en: Some colleagues stayed late.
items.note: algum, alguma, alguns, algumas

activities.title: Uns e outros
activities.intro: Practise picking the right indefinite and making it agree.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ pessoa paga a sua parte da conta.'
questions.explanation: Cada is the only one that fits before a singular noun with a distributive meaning.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is grammatically correct?
options: Cada os alunos trouxe um caderno.
questions.explanation: Cada is invariable, takes a singular noun with no article, and the verb stays singular.

questions.prompt: Complete so the sentence says an unnamed person is waiting at reception: '___ está à tua espera na receção.'
questions.explanation: Alguém replaces a person whose identity is not given.

questions.prompt: Complete with an indefinite that agrees with cidades: 'Já visitei ___ cidades do norte, não só o Porto.'
questions.explanation: Cidades is feminine plural, so the indefinite must be feminine plural too.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Do you want to eat something before leaving?'
questions.explanation: Alguma coisa follows the verb it is the object of, and antes de is followed by the infinitive.

activities.title: A organizar o jantar da equipa
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a colleague sort out an office dinner using indefinites.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alguém já reservou a mesa para sexta-feira?
lines.en: Has anyone booked the table for Friday yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda não. Telefono eu a vários restaurantes hoje.
lines.en: Not yet. I'll call several restaurants today.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quanto é que cada pessoa vai pagar?
lines.en: And how much is each person going to pay?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cada colega paga vinte euros, bebidas incluídas.
lines.en: Each colleague pays twenty euros, drinks included.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alguns colegas são vegetarianos, não te esqueças.
lines.en: Some colleagues are vegetarian, don't forget.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Peço sempre alguma coisa sem carne para eles.
lines.en: I always ask for something without meat for them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Precisas de ajuda com alguma coisa?
lines.en: Do you need help with anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Só se alguém puder levar o bolo.
lines.en: Only if someone can bring the cake.

days.title: Verbo + preposição + infinitivo: começar a, acabar de, tentar
days.objective: You can chain two verbs together with the right preposition to say you start, have just finished, stop, continue or try to do something.
days.paretoFocus: The preposition is fixed by the first verb and cannot be guessed from English, so learning these few patterns removes a very frequent source of mistakes.
drills: Say three things you have just done, using acabar de.
Describe when you started three of your current habits with começar a.
Write two sentences with deixar de about habits you gave up.

items: Which preposition follows começar and continuar?
Which structure means to have just done something?
Which of today's verbs takes the infinitive with no preposition at all?

activities.title: Dois verbos seguidos
activities.intro: When two verbs follow each other, the first one decides the preposition. Começar and continuar take a, acabar and deixar take de, and tentar takes the infinitive directly. Acabar de plus infinitive means to have just done something.

items.hr: Comecei a aprender português no ano passado.
items.en: I started learning Portuguese last year.
items.note: começar a + infinitive

items.hr: Acabei de chegar a casa.
items.en: I have just got home.
items.note: acabar de = to have just done something

items.hr: Vou tentar sair mais cedo hoje.
items.en: I'm going to try to leave earlier today.
items.note: tentar takes no preposition

items.hr: Ela deixou de fumar em janeiro.
items.en: She stopped smoking in January.
items.note: deixar de + infinitive = to give up doing

items.hr: Continuo a estudar à noite.
items.en: I keep on studying in the evening.
items.note: continuar a, not continuar de

activities.title: A preposição certa
activities.intro: Practise linking two verbs with the preposition each one requires.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ele ___ trabalhar às nove da manhã.'
questions.explanation: Começar always takes a before an infinitive.

questions.prompt: What does 'Acabei de comer' mean?
questions.explanation: Acabar de plus infinitive places the action a moment before now.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: 'Ela deixou ___ beber café à noite.'
questions.explanation: Deixar takes de before an infinitive when it means to give something up.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb that means to make an attempt: 'Vou ___ resolver isto hoje.'
questions.explanation: This verb is followed directly by the infinitive, with no preposition.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I have just spoken to the doctor.'
questions.explanation: Acabar goes in the past, then de, then the infinitive.

activities.title: Novos hábitos
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about your new routine and you answer with verb plus preposition plus infinitive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ouvi dizer que começaste a correr de manhã.
lines.en: I heard you started running in the mornings.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comecei a correr há duas semanas, às sete.
lines.en: I started running two weeks ago, at seven.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o café? Bebias imenso.
lines.en: And coffee? You used to drink a lot.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Deixei de beber café depois do almoço.
lines.en: I stopped drinking coffee after lunch.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Impressionante. Vais continuar assim no inverno?
lines.en: Impressive. Are you going to keep it up in the winter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vou tentar continuar a correr, mesmo com chuva.
lines.en: I'm going to try to keep running, even in the rain.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hoje já correste?
lines.en: Have you run today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Acabei de chegar, corri seis quilómetros.
lines.en: I've just got back, I ran six kilometres.

days.title: A obrigação: ter de, dever, é preciso
days.objective: You can express personal obligation, advice and impersonal necessity with ter de, dever and é preciso.
days.paretoFocus: Obligation comes up constantly in work, paperwork and travel, and Portuguese separates a personal duty from a general requirement in a way English often does not.
drills: List five things you have to do this week, each with ter de.
Give three pieces of advice to a friend using dever.
Turn two of your ter de sentences into impersonal sentences with é preciso.

items: Which preposition follows ter when it expresses obligation in Portugal?
Which structure has no subject and states a general requirement?
Say in Portuguese: you should rest more.

activities.title: Tenho de ir
activities.intro: Ter de plus infinitive is the everyday way to express a personal obligation in Portugal. Dever plus infinitive is softer and gives advice or a recommendation. É preciso plus infinitive states a requirement with no particular person responsible.

items.hr: Tenho de ir ao banco antes das três.
items.en: I have to go to the bank before three.
items.note: ter de + infinitive, the standard form in Portugal

items.hr: Deves descansar mais e dormir cedo.
items.en: You should rest more and sleep early.
items.note: dever = should, advice rather than duty

items.hr: É preciso levar o cartão de cidadão.
items.en: It is necessary to bring your identity card.
items.note: é preciso is impersonal, no subject

items.hr: Não é preciso pagar já.
items.en: There is no need to pay right now.
items.note: the negative removes the requirement

items.hr: Temos de apanhar o comboio das sete.
items.en: We have to catch the seven o'clock train.
items.note: ter agrees with the person, the infinitive does not

activities.title: O que é preciso fazer
activities.intro: Practise choosing between personal obligation, advice and impersonal necessity.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Não te sentes bem? ___ ir ao médico esta tarde.'
questions.explanation: The person addressed is tu, so dever takes the second person singular deves.

questions.prompt: Which sentence states a requirement without naming who is responsible?
questions.explanation: É preciso is impersonal, while ter de and dever are conjugated for a subject.

questions.prompt: Complete with the preposition that follows ter in an obligation: 'Ela tem ___ trabalhar no próximo sábado.'
questions.explanation: In Portugal the obligation structure is ter de plus infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb so the sentence states that validating the ticket is necessary: '___ preciso validar o bilhete.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal structure uses the third person singular of ser.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'We have to book a table for tonight.'
questions.explanation: Ter is conjugated for nós, then de, then the infinitive.

activities.title: Tratar dos papéis
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague explains what is required to renew a document and you talk about your own obligations.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sabes o que é preciso para renovar o passaporte?
lines.en: Do you know what is needed to renew a passport?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É preciso marcar hora e levar uma fotografia.
lines.en: You need to book an appointment and bring a photograph.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tenho de ir lá pessoalmente?
lines.en: Do I have to go there in person?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens, e deves chegar dez minutos antes.
lines.en: You do, and you should arrive ten minutes early.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E é preciso pagar no próprio dia?
lines.en: And is it necessary to pay on the day itself?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não é preciso, podes pagar quando levantares o documento.
lines.en: It isn't necessary, you can pay when you collect the document.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vens comigo na quinta-feira?
lines.en: Will you come with me on Thursday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não posso, tenho de acabar um relatório até sexta.
lines.en: I can't, I have to finish a report by Friday.

days.title: Os pronomes possessivos e a concordância
days.objective: You can say who something belongs to, making the possessive agree with the thing owned and using the article the way Portugal does.
days.paretoFocus: Portuguese possessives agree with the object, not the owner, and European Portuguese normally puts an article in front of them, two habits that are easy to fix once and use forever.
drills: Point at ten objects around you and say whose they are.
Write five sentences with dele and dela instead of seu, to avoid ambiguity.
Say three sentences with ser plus a possessive and notice that the article disappears.

items: Does the possessive agree with the owner or with the thing owned?
When do you drop the article before a possessive?
Say in Portuguese: my parents live in Coimbra.

activities.title: De quem é isto?
activities.intro: The possessive agrees in gender and number with the thing owned, not with the owner. In Portugal an article normally comes before it: o meu carro, a nossa casa. After the verb ser the article is dropped, and dele or dela replaces seu when you want to be clear about who the owner is.

items.hr: Este é o meu telemóvel e aquele é o teu.
items.en: This is my mobile and that one is yours.
items.note: the article stays: o meu, o teu

items.hr: A nossa casa é perto da vossa.
items.en: Our house is near yours.
items.note: nossa and vossa agree with casa, which is feminine

items.hr: Estas chaves são minhas.
items.en: These keys are mine.
items.note: after ser the article disappears

items.hr: O carro dele está avariado.
items.en: His car has broken down.
items.note: dele and dela avoid the ambiguity of seu

items.hr: As tuas malas já estão no quarto.
items.en: Your suitcases are already in the room.
items.note: malas is feminine plural, so tuas

activities.title: É meu ou é teu?
activities.intro: Practise the agreement of possessives and the use of the article.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Esta mochila não é minha, é ___.'
questions.explanation: Mochila is feminine singular, so the possessive is feminine singular, with no article after ser.

questions.prompt: Complete, talking about your own family: '___ pais vivem no Porto.'
questions.explanation: Pais is masculine plural and European Portuguese keeps the article before the possessive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the possessive that agrees with irmã and belongs to us: 'A ___ irmã trabalha connosco.'
questions.explanation: Irmã is feminine singular, so the possessive takes the feminine singular form.

questions.prompt: Complete so it is clear the jacket belongs to him and not to you: 'O casaco ___ ficou no carro.'
questions.explanation: De plus ele gives dele, which names the owner unambiguously.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Is this jacket yours?'
questions.explanation: After ser the possessive stands alone with no article and agrees with casaco.

activities.title: Perdido e achado no ginásio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone at the lost property desk asks about objects and you say who they belong to.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Este guarda-chuva é seu?
lines.en: Good afternoon. Is this umbrella yours?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não é meu, mas o casaco azul é meu.
lines.en: It isn't mine, but the blue jacket is mine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E estas chaves? Estavam ao lado do casaco.
lines.en: And these keys? They were next to the jacket.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Essas são da minha colega, são dela.
lines.en: Those are my colleague's, they're hers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Também temos uns óculos e uma garrafa.
lines.en: We also have some glasses and a bottle.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A garrafa é nossa, os óculos não são meus.
lines.en: The bottle is ours, the glasses aren't mine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Precisa de assinar aqui para levar as suas coisas.
lines.en: You need to sign here to take your things.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Com certeza. O meu nome está no cartão.
lines.en: Of course. My name is on the card.

days.title: Os pronomes tónicos: mim, ti, si, consigo
days.objective: You can use the stressed pronouns that follow prepositions, including the special forms comigo, contigo, connosco and consigo.
days.paretoFocus: After any preposition Portuguese switches to a different set of pronouns, so para eu and com ti are immediately wrong; the correct forms are few and used constantly.
drills: Say five sentences with para plus a stressed pronoun, changing person each time.
Invite three different people somewhere, using comigo, contigo and connosco.
Practise the polite form consigo in three sentences addressing a stranger.

items: Which pronouns replace eu and tu after a preposition?
What happens when com meets mim, ti or nós?
Which form do you use with someone you address formally?

activities.title: Para mim, contigo
activities.intro: After a preposition Portuguese uses stressed pronouns: mim, ti, ele, ela, si, nós, eles, elas. With com there are fused forms: comigo, contigo, connosco and consigo. Consigo is the polite way of saying with you to someone you address as o senhor or a senhora.

items.hr: Isto é para ti.
items.en: This is for you.
items.note: after a preposition you say ti, never tu

items.hr: Ela falou de mim ao chefe.
items.en: She spoke about me to the boss.
items.note: de mim, never de eu

items.hr: Queres vir ao cinema comigo?
items.en: Do you want to come to the cinema with me?
items.note: com + mim gives comigo

items.hr: Posso falar consigo um momento?
items.en: May I speak with you for a moment?
items.note: consigo is the polite form of contigo
