items.hr: Eles vêm connosco no carro.
items.en: They are coming with us in the car.
items.note: com + nós gives connosco

activities.title: Depois da preposição
activities.intro: Practise the stressed pronouns and the fused forms with com.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Este presente é para ___.'
questions.explanation: After a preposition the first person becomes mim, never eu.

questions.prompt: Complete, speaking to a friend: 'Não te preocupes, eu vou ___.'
questions.explanation: Com and ti fuse into a single word, contigo.

questions.prompt: Complete politely, speaking to Dona Ana: 'Posso falar ___ depois da reunião?'
questions.explanation: The polite equivalent of contigo is consigo, built on the stressed pronoun si.

questions.prompt: Complete with the stressed pronoun for the group you belong to: 'Eles não querem ir sem ___.'
questions.explanation: After sem the first person plural keeps the form nós; only com would give connosco.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'She wants to have dinner with us on Saturday.'
questions.explanation: Com plus nós gives the single word connosco, placed after the infinitive.

activities.title: Um convite para o fim de semana
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour invites you out and you answer using stressed pronouns after prepositions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Olá! Vamos à praia no sábado. Queres vir connosco?
lines.en: Hi! We're going to the beach on Saturday. Do you want to come with us?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Adorava ir convosco, mas trabalho de manhã.
lines.en: I'd love to go with you, but I work in the morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sem problema. Podemos esperar por ti até à uma.
lines.en: No problem. We can wait for you until one.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então vou com vocês. Levo comigo uma salada.
lines.en: Then I'll go with you. I'll take a salad with me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o teu vizinho? A boleia é para ele também?
lines.en: And your neighbour? Is the lift for him too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Falo com ele hoje e digo-te alguma coisa.
lines.en: I'll speak to him today and let you know.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Combinado. Contamos contigo, então.
lines.en: Agreed. We're counting on you, then.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Podem contar comigo, chego à uma em ponto.
lines.en: You can count on me, I'll be there at one sharp.

days.title: As horas e os compromissos do dia
days.objective: You can tell the time precisely and arrange or change appointments during the day.
days.paretoFocus: Every appointment, ticket and meeting depends on saying the time correctly, and Portuguese uses é for one o'clock and são for all the others.
drills: Say aloud twelve different times, half of them with e meia or e um quarto.
Write out your timetable for tomorrow, one sentence per appointment.
Practise three ways of proposing a time and three ways of changing it.

items: When do you use é instead of são to tell the time?
Which preposition introduces the time of an event?
Say in Portuguese: the meeting starts at half past nine.

activities.title: A que horas?
activities.intro: Use são for all hours except one o'clock and noon and midnight, which take é. To say when something happens, use às plus the hour, or ao meio-dia. Marcar is the verb for making an appointment, and por volta de means around a given time.

items.hr: São três e meia da tarde.
items.en: It's half past three in the afternoon.
items.note: são for two o'clock and beyond

items.hr: É uma e um quarto.
items.en: It's a quarter past one.
items.note: é uma hora, é meio-dia, é meia-noite

items.hr: A reunião é às nove em ponto.
items.en: The meeting is at nine sharp.
items.note: às + hour, em ponto = sharp

items.hr: Marquei o dentista para as cinco da tarde.
items.en: I made a dentist appointment for five in the afternoon.
items.note: marcar para + hour

items.hr: Estou atrasado, chego por volta das oito.
items.en: I'm late, I'll arrive around eight.
items.note: por volta de + article + hour

activities.title: Que horas são?
activities.intro: Practise telling the time and fixing appointments.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ uma hora da tarde.'
questions.explanation: One o'clock is singular, so ser stays in the singular: é uma hora.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A loja abre ___ nove da manhã.'
questions.explanation: The time of an event takes a plus the feminine plural article, written às.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of ser: '___ oito e vinte, temos de sair.'
questions.explanation: From two o'clock onwards the verb is plural: são oito e vinte.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb used for booking a medical appointment: 'Preciso de ___ uma consulta para segunda-feira.'
questions.explanation: Marcar uma consulta is the fixed expression for arranging an appointment.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The meeting starts at half past nine.'
questions.explanation: Às introduces the hour, and e meia follows the number.

activities.title: A remarcar a consulta
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You phone a clinic to change the time of an appointment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde, fala da clínica dentária. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.en: Good afternoon, this is the dental clinic. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Tenho consulta amanhã às dez e meia.
lines.en: Good afternoon. I have an appointment tomorrow at half past ten.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Confirmo. Quer manter essa hora?
lines.en: I can confirm that. Do you want to keep that time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Queria remarcar para as cinco da tarde, se for possível.
lines.en: I'd like to rebook for five in the afternoon, if possible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Às cinco está ocupado. Pode ser às seis menos um quarto?
lines.en: Five is taken. Can it be at a quarter to six?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pode, sim. Consigo chegar por volta dessa hora.
lines.en: Yes, it can. I can get there around that time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Muito bem. Chegue dez minutos antes, por favor.
lines.en: Very well. Please arrive ten minutes early.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado, estarei aí às cinco e meia em ponto.
lines.en: Agreed, I'll be there at half past five sharp.

days.title: No banco e nos correios
days.objective: You can carry out basic bank and post office transactions: withdrawing money, opening an account, sending a letter or a parcel.
days.paretoFocus: These two counters use a small, fixed set of phrases, and knowing them means you can handle money and post in Portugal without switching to English.
drills: Rehearse three polite requests beginning with Queria.
Say aloud what you would ask to send a registered letter abroad.
Practise asking the price of three different postal services.

items: Which polite verb form softens a request at a counter?
What is the everyday Portuguese word for a cash machine?
Say in Portuguese: I would like to withdraw money, please.

activities.title: Ao balcão
activities.intro: At a counter, Portuguese speakers soften requests with queria, which is the imperfect of querer used as a polite conditional. Levantar and depositar are the verbs for taking out and paying in money, and multibanco is the everyday word for the cash machine and the card network.

items.hr: Queria levantar cem euros, por favor.
items.en: I'd like to withdraw a hundred euros, please.
items.note: queria is politer than quero

items.hr: Onde posso abrir uma conta?
items.en: Where can I open an account?
items.note: abrir uma conta, fechar uma conta

items.hr: Quanto custa enviar esta carta para França?
items.en: How much does it cost to send this letter to France?
items.note: enviar or mandar, both are used

items.hr: Queria mandar esta carta registada.
items.en: I'd like to send this letter by registered post.
items.note: carta registada gives you proof of delivery

items.hr: O multibanco não aceitou o meu cartão.
items.en: The cash machine didn't accept my card.
items.note: multibanco is the word used all over Portugal

activities.title: Dinheiro e cartas
activities.intro: Practise the verbs and nouns you need at the bank and at the post office.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Queria ___ dinheiro da minha conta.'
questions.explanation: Levantar dinheiro is the fixed expression for withdrawing cash.

questions.prompt: You want proof that your letter was delivered. What do you ask for?
questions.explanation: Registada means the letter is tracked and signed for on delivery.

questions.prompt: Complete with the Portuguese word for a cash machine: 'Há um ___ ali, ao lado do supermercado.'
questions.explanation: In Portugal the machine and the card system are both called multibanco.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb that starts a new bank account: 'Queria ___ uma conta neste banco.'
questions.explanation: Abrir uma conta is the standard collocation.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'How much does it cost to send this parcel to Spain?'
questions.explanation: Quanto custa is followed directly by the infinitive of the action.

activities.title: Uma encomenda para o estrangeiro
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are at the post office counter sending a parcel and asking about payment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Diga, faz favor.
lines.en: Good afternoon. Go ahead, please.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Queria enviar esta encomenda para Itália.
lines.en: I'd like to send this parcel to Italy.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pesa dois quilos. Quer envio normal ou registado?
lines.en: It weighs two kilos. Do you want normal or registered postage?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Registado, por favor. Quanto tempo demora?
lines.en: Registered, please. How long does it take?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cerca de cinco dias úteis. São dezoito euros.
lines.en: About five working days. That's eighteen euros.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Posso pagar com multibanco ou tem de ser em dinheiro?
lines.en: Can I pay by card or does it have to be cash?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pode pagar com cartão. Precisa de mais alguma coisa?
lines.en: You can pay by card. Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Queria também dois selos para postais, se faz favor.
lines.en: I'd also like two stamps for postcards, please.

days.title: As compras online: encomendar, entregar, trocar
days.objective: You can order goods, follow a delivery, and ask to exchange or return an item that is wrong.
days.paretoFocus: Online shopping problems always come down to the same four verbs, and being able to state them clearly is what gets an order fixed.
drills: Describe an order you placed recently in three sentences, from ordering to delivery.
Write two complaints: one about a wrong size, one about a late parcel.
Practise asking to exchange an item and to get your money back.

items: Which verb do you use for clothes that do not fit you?
What is the difference between trocar and devolver?
Say in Portuguese: I ordered a coat last week.

activities.title: A minha encomenda
activities.intro: Encomendar is to place an order, entregar is to deliver it, trocar is to swap an item for another one, and devolver is to send it back for a refund. Servir is the verb used for clothes and shoes that fit or do not fit you.

items.hr: Encomendei um casaco na semana passada.
items.en: I ordered a coat last week.
items.note: encomendar, uma encomenda

items.hr: O estafeta entrega a encomenda amanhã de manhã.
items.en: The courier delivers the parcel tomorrow morning.
items.note: o estafeta is the delivery driver

items.hr: Este casaco não me serve, quero trocá-lo por um maior.
items.en: This coat doesn't fit me, I want to exchange it for a bigger one.
items.note: trocar algo por algo, with the pronoun attached to the infinitive

items.hr: Posso devolver o artigo no prazo de catorze dias.
items.en: I can return the item within fourteen days.
items.note: devolver leads to a refund, trocar to an exchange

items.hr: Já recebi a confirmação da encomenda.
items.en: I have already received the order confirmation.
items.note: receber a confirmação, o número da encomenda

activities.title: Trocas e devoluções
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of ordering, delivery and returns.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A camisola é muito pequena, quero ___ por outra maior.'
questions.explanation: Camisola is feminine, and the pronoun a attached to the infinitive gives trocá-la.

questions.prompt: Which verb means to send an item back to the shop and get your money back?
questions.explanation: Devolver is to return the goods; entregar is what the courier does.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb used for shoes that are the wrong size: 'Estes sapatos não me ___, são um número abaixo do meu.'
questions.explanation: Servir agrees with sapatos, which is plural, so the form is servem.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb for placing an order: 'Vou ___ o livro esta noite.'
questions.explanation: The noun encomenda comes from this verb, which means to order goods.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The courier delivered the parcel yesterday.'
questions.explanation: Entregar in the pretérito perfeito gives entregou for the third person singular.

activities.title: O tamanho errado
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call customer service because the item you ordered is the wrong size.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Apoio ao cliente, bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.en: Customer service, good morning. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Encomendei umas calças e não me servem.
lines.en: Good morning. I ordered some trousers and they don't fit me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lamento. Quer trocar por outro tamanho ou devolver?
lines.en: I'm sorry. Do you want to exchange them for another size or return them?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Queria trocá-las por um tamanho maior.
lines.en: I'd like to exchange them for a bigger size.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sem problema. Tem o número da encomenda?
lines.en: No problem. Do you have the order number?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tenho, está no e-mail de confirmação que recebi.
lines.en: I do, it's in the confirmation email I received.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ótimo. O estafeta passa por sua casa na quinta-feira.
lines.en: Great. The courier will come by your house on Thursday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E quando é que entregam as novas calças?
lines.en: And when will you deliver the new trousers?

days.title: Escrever um mél informal: convite, agradecimento, desculpa
days.objective: Write a short informal e-mail in European Portuguese that invites someone, thanks them or apologises, with a natural opening and sign-off.
days.paretoFocus: Nearly every message you send to a Portuguese friend does one of these three jobs, and the opening and closing formulas are reused word for word every time.
drills: Write a five line e-mail inviting a friend to your birthday, with an informal greeting and sign-off.
Write two thank you sentences for a dinner, one as a man and one as a woman.
Write an apology for a late reply using peço desculpa por plus an infinitive.

items: Say three informal ways to open and three ways to close a message to a friend.
How does por combine with o, a, os and as? Give the four contracted forms.
Which ending does a woman use when she thanks someone: obrigado or obrigada?

activities.title: Um mél para um amigo
activities.intro: An informal e-mail in Portugal opens with the person's name and a quick question about how they are, then states its purpose in one line. Thanks use obrigado or obrigada plus por, which contracts with the article: por + o gives pelo, por + a gives pela. Apologies use peço desculpa por followed by an infinitive.

items.hr: Olá, Rita! Tudo bem contigo?
items.en: Hi Rita! How are things with you?
items.note: contigo = with you, the form of com plus tu

items.hr: Escrevo-te para te convidar para o meu aniversário.
items.en: I am writing to invite you to my birthday.
items.note: escrevo-te: in a plain affirmative sentence the pronoun follows the verb

items.hr: Muito obrigada pelo jantar de sábado, correu tudo muito bem.
items.en: Thank you so much for Saturday's dinner, everything went really well.
items.note: pelo = por + o; obrigada because a woman is writing

items.hr: Peço desculpa por não ter respondido mais cedo.
items.en: I am sorry for not having replied sooner.
items.note: peço desculpa por + infinitive

items.hr: Um beijinho e até breve, Ana.
items.en: A kiss and see you soon, Ana.
items.note: Um beijinho is a normal sign-off between friends in Portugal

activities.title: Convidar, agradecer, pedir desculpa
activities.intro: Practise the informal openings, the contractions with por and the apology formula.

questions.prompt: Which opening fits an informal e-mail to a close friend?
questions.explanation: The other three belong to formal writing. An informal message opens with the name and a quick question about the person.

questions.prompt: A friend cooked you dinner and you are a woman. Which sentence thanks her correctly?
questions.explanation: Thanks take por, never para, and por + o contracts to pelo. Obrigada agrees with the woman who is speaking.

questions.prompt: Apologise politely: 'Peço ____ por ter chegado tarde.'
questions.explanation: Peço desculpa por is the standard apology formula in European Portuguese.

questions.prompt: Contract the preposition with the article: 'Obrigado ____ (por + as) fotografias.'
questions.explanation: por + as gives pelas. The full set is pelo, pela, pelos, pelas.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I am inviting you to dinner at my house on Saturday.'
questions.explanation: Convido-te keeps the pronoun attached after the verb, and no sábado gives the day.

activities.title: O convite que te mandei
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend follows up on the e-mail she sent you, and you thank her, apologise and propose another day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Recebeste o mél que te mandei ontem?
lines.en: Did you get the e-mail I sent you yesterday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Recebi, sim. Obrigado pelo convite para o teu aniversário.
lines.en: Yes, I did. Thank you for the invitation to your birthday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, podes vir no sábado à noite?
lines.en: So, can you come on Saturday evening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Peço desculpa, mas no sábado estou fora. Posso passar no domingo?
lines.en: I am sorry, but I am away on Saturday. Can I drop by on Sunday?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Claro, o domingo também dá. Respondes-me por escrito?
lines.en: Of course, Sunday works too. Will you reply to me in writing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Respondo-te logo à noite. Um beijinho e até domingo.
lines.en: I will reply to you tonight. A kiss and see you on Sunday.

days.title: Contar o fim de semana por ordem
days.objective: Tell someone what you did at the weekend as an ordered story, using primeiro, depois, a seguir, mais tarde and por fim with past tense verbs.
days.paretoFocus: Monday morning small talk is the same conversation everywhere, and a handful of sequencing words turns a list of verbs into a story people can follow.
drills: Write five sentences about last Saturday, each one starting with a different sequencing word.
Retell the same weekend out loud in under thirty seconds, keeping the order clear.
List five things you did yesterday and put them in order from morning to night.

items: Name four connectors that place an event in a sequence, from first to last.
Give the eu and nós past forms of ir, fazer and apanhar.
Which connector closes a story and which one opens it?

activities.title: Primeiro, depois, por fim
activities.intro: To narrate a weekend, Portuguese speakers chain past events with a small set of ordering words placed at the start of the sentence, usually followed by a comma. Primeiro opens the sequence, depois and a seguir move it along, mais tarde jumps forward, and por fim closes it. The verbs stay in the pretérito perfeito, the tense for finished events.

items.hr: Primeiro, acordei tarde e tomei o pequeno-almoço com calma.
items.en: First, I woke up late and had breakfast slowly.
items.note: pequeno-almoço = breakfast in Portugal

items.hr: Depois, apanhei o comboio para o Porto.
items.en: Then I caught the train to Porto.
items.note: apanhar o comboio = to catch the train

items.hr: A seguir, encontrei-me com uns amigos no centro.
items.en: Next, I met up with some friends in the centre.
items.note: encontrar-se com alguém = to meet up with someone

items.hr: Mais tarde, fomos todos jantar a um restaurante perto do rio.
items.en: Later, we all went for dinner at a restaurant near the river.
items.note: fomos is the past of both ir and ser

items.hr: Por fim, voltei a casa por volta da meia-noite.
items.en: Finally, I got home around midnight.
items.note: por volta de = around, with clock times

activities.title: Pôr a história por ordem
activities.intro: Practise the ordering words and the past forms that carry the story.

questions.prompt: Which word opens a sequence of events?
questions.explanation: Primeiro marks the first event. The other three only make sense once something has already happened.

questions.prompt: Complete the last sentence of a story: '____, apanhámos o autocarro de volta e chegámos exaustos.'
questions.explanation: Por fim closes a narration. The other options all announce a first step.

questions.prompt: Put the verb in the past: 'No sábado, eu ____ (ir) à praia com a minha irmã.'
questions.explanation: Ir is irregular in the pretérito perfeito: fui, foste, foi, fomos, foram.

questions.prompt: Complete with the nós past of apanhar: 'Depois, ____ o comboio das oito.'
questions.explanation: European Portuguese writes the nós past of -ar verbs with an acute accent, apanhámos, to separate it from the present.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Then we met up with some friends in the city centre.'
questions.explanation: Encontrámos loses its final s before the pronoun nos, giving encontrámo-nos.

activities.title: Como correu o teu fim de semana?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks about your weekend on Monday morning and you narrate it in order.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como correu o teu fim de semana?
lines.en: So, how was your weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Correu muito bem. Primeiro, fui à praia no sábado de manhã.
lines.en: It went really well. First, I went to the beach on Saturday morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E depois? Ficaste por lá o dia todo?
lines.en: And then? Did you stay there all day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, a seguir apanhei o comboio para Cascais e almocei com a minha prima.
lines.en: No, next I caught the train to Cascais and had lunch with my cousin.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E no domingo, fizeste alguma coisa?
lines.en: And on Sunday, did you do anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mais tarde, no domingo, vi um filme em casa. Por fim, deitei-me cedo.
lines.en: Later on Sunday I watched a film at home. Finally, I went to bed early.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que inveja. Eu trabalhei os dois dias.
lines.en: I am jealous. I worked both days.

days.title: Descrever um lugar: a cidade, o bairro, o campo
days.objective: Describe where a place is and what it is like, using ficar for location, há for existence and adjectives such as calmo, movimentado and antigo.
days.paretoFocus: People ask where you live within minutes of meeting you, and ficar plus há covers almost every sentence you need to answer well.
drills: Describe your own neighbourhood in four sentences, one with ficar and two with há.
List six adjectives for places and pair each one with its opposite.
Describe a village and a big city using the same sentence pattern twice.

items: Which verb gives the location of a fixed place, and which one says that something exists there?
Say the opposites of movimentado, barulhento and moderno.
How do you say rush hour and narrow streets?

activities.title: Onde fica e como é
activities.intro: For a fixed place, location is given by ficar, not by estar: o bairro fica perto do centro. What exists in that place is introduced by há, which never changes for plural. Character is described with ser plus adjectives, because it is a permanent trait of the place.

items.hr: O meu bairro fica perto do centro, mas é bastante calmo.
items.en: My neighbourhood is near the centre, but it is quite quiet.
items.note: ficar = to be located, for places that do not move

items.hr: Há muitos cafés e uma praça com esplanadas.
items.en: There are lots of cafés and a square with outdoor terraces.
items.note: há is invariable: there is and there are

items.hr: É uma zona antiga, com ruas estreitas e prédios baixos.
items.en: It is an old area, with narrow streets and low buildings.
items.note: prédio = apartment block

items.hr: No campo, o ar é puro e ouve-se o silêncio à noite.
items.en: In the countryside the air is clean and you can hear the silence at night.
items.note: ouve-se: the se makes the sentence impersonal

items.hr: A cidade é movimentada e há muito trânsito nas horas de ponta.
items.en: The city is busy and there is a lot of traffic at rush hour.
items.note: hora de ponta = rush hour

activities.title: A cidade, o bairro e o campo
activities.intro: Practise locating a place and saying what it has and what it is like.

questions.prompt: Which verb says where a fixed place is located?
questions.explanation: Ficar gives the permanent position of a place: a estação fica ali.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'No meu bairro ____ duas escolas e um mercado.'
questions.explanation: Existence is expressed with há, which stays singular even with a plural noun.

questions.prompt: Complete with the opposite of movimentada: 'A aldeia é pequena e muito ____.'
questions.explanation: The adjective agrees with aldeia, which is feminine, so the ending is -a.

questions.prompt: Complete the rush hour expression: 'Há muito trânsito nas horas de ____.'
questions.explanation: Hora de ponta is the fixed expression for rush hour in Portugal.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The village is quiet and there are only two cafés.'
questions.explanation: Ser describes the character of the place and há introduces what exists in it.

activities.title: Onde é que tu vives?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone you have just met asks about your area and you describe where it is and what it is like.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vives no centro ou nos arredores?
lines.en: Do you live in the centre or on the outskirts?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vivo num bairro antigo que fica a dez minutos do centro.
lines.en: I live in an old neighbourhood that is ten minutes from the centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E como é? É barulhento?
lines.en: And what is it like? Is it noisy?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, é bastante sossegado. Há uma praça com esplanadas e muitas árvores.
lines.en: No, it is quite quiet. There is a square with terraces and a lot of trees.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Eu cresci no campo. Aqui na cidade há sempre trânsito.
lines.en: I grew up in the countryside. Here in the city there is always traffic.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Percebo. No campo o ar é puro, mas há poucos transportes.
lines.en: I understand. In the countryside the air is clean, but there is little public transport.

days.title: Gostos e preferências: exprimir e justificar
days.objective: Say what you love, prefer or cannot stand and give a reason for it, using gostar de, preferir A a B, não suportar and porque or por isso.
days.paretoFocus: An opinion without a reason ends a conversation, and the preposition traps in gostar de and preferir a are the two mistakes learners repeat for years.
drills: Write four sentences about your tastes, each with a reason introduced by porque.
Write three preferences using preferir with the second item after a.
Say one thing you cannot stand and add a consequence with por isso.

items: Which preposition always follows gostar, and which one introduces the rejected option after preferir?
Say a strong like and a strong dislike without using gostar.
What is the difference in position between porque and por isso in a sentence?

activities.title: Do que é que gostas mais?
activities.intro: Gostar is never used alone: it always takes de before the thing you like, even before a verb. Preferir compares two things with the pattern preferir A a B, where a contracts with the article, giving ao or à. A reason follows porque, while por isso introduces the consequence instead.

items.hr: Adoro música ao vivo, porque o ambiente é muito melhor.
items.en: I love live music, because the atmosphere is much better.
items.note: adorar is stronger than gostar and needs no preposition

items.hr: Prefiro ler a ver televisão.
items.en: I prefer reading to watching television.
items.note: preferir A a B: the rejected option takes a

items.hr: Não suporto filmes muito longos.
items.en: I cannot stand very long films.
items.note: não suportar = cannot stand

items.hr: O que eu gosto mais é de passear ao fim da tarde.
items.en: What I like most is going for a walk in the late afternoon.
items.note: gostar keeps de even when the sentence is reordered for emphasis

items.hr: Detesto acordar cedo, por isso trabalho à tarde.
items.en: I hate getting up early, so I work in the afternoon.
items.note: por isso introduces the result, not the reason

activities.title: Dizer o que gosto e porquê
activities.intro: Practise the prepositions after gostar and preferir and the two ways of linking a reason.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Prefiro o comboio ____ carro.'
questions.explanation: Preferir uses a before the rejected option, and a + o contracts to ao.

questions.prompt: Which sentence says that you really cannot stand something?
questions.explanation: Não suportar expresses strong rejection. The other three are all positive.

questions.prompt: Gostar always needs one small word after it. Complete: 'Gosto muito ____ música portuguesa.'
questions.explanation: Gostar is always followed by de before its object, with no exceptions.

questions.prompt: Add the reason: 'Adoro o outono ____ não está muito calor.'
questions.explanation: Porque introduces the cause. The consequence would need por isso instead.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I cannot stand very long films, I prefer series.'
questions.explanation: Muito before an adjective is invariable, and the adjective longos agrees with filmes.

activities.title: Gostos e razões
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about your tastes and you answer with a preference and a justification each time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gostas de música ao vivo?
lines.en: Do you like live music?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Adoro, sobretudo jazz, porque o ambiente é mais íntimo.
lines.en: I love it, especially jazz, because the atmosphere is more intimate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E teatro? Eu acho um bocado aborrecido.
lines.en: And theatre? I find it a bit boring.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prefiro cinema a teatro, mas não suporto filmes muito longos.
lines.en: I prefer cinema to theatre, but I cannot stand very long films.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então o que é que gostas mais de fazer ao fim de semana?
lines.en: So what do you like doing most at the weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O que eu gosto mais é de passear, porque a cidade fica calma.
lines.en: What I like most is going for a walk, because the city gets quiet.

days.title: Dar conselhos e reagir a um problema
days.objective: React to a friend's problem with sympathy and then give practical advice using devias, porque não and o melhor é.
days.paretoFocus: Advice is the most common reason friends talk at all, and devias plus an infinitive gives you a soft, native sounding suggestion without touching the subjunctive.
drills: Write three reactions to bad news that are not simply que pena.
Give the same piece of advice three times, once with devias, once as a question and once with o melhor é.
Describe a small problem of your own and write two possible pieces of advice for it.

items: Say two short reactions that show sympathy for a problem.
Which verb form softens advice into you should, and what follows it?
How do you turn advice into a question that suggests an action?

activities.title: Que chatice! Devias falar com ele
activities.intro: Portuguese advice usually starts with a reaction, because giving advice straight away sounds cold. Devias, the imperfect of dever, plus an infinitive is the standard soft suggestion, equivalent to you should. Porque não plus a present tense verb turns the advice into a friendly question, and o melhor é plus an infinitive presents it as the obvious solution.

items.hr: Que chatice! Lamento muito.
items.en: What a nuisance! I am really sorry.
items.note: Que chatice is the everyday reaction to annoying news

items.hr: Devias ir ao médico o mais depressa possível.
items.en: You should go to the doctor as soon as possible.
items.note: devias + infinitive = you should

items.hr: Porque não falas com o senhorio?
items.en: Why don't you talk to the landlord?
items.note: porque não + present tense makes a suggestion

items.hr: O melhor é telefonar amanhã de manhã.
items.en: The best thing is to phone tomorrow morning.
items.note: o melhor é + infinitive

items.hr: Não te preocupes, isso resolve-se.
items.en: Do not worry, that will sort itself out.
items.note: resolve-se: the se makes the verb reflexive and impersonal

activities.title: Reagir e aconselhar
activities.intro: Practise choosing a reaction and shaping advice three different ways.

questions.prompt: Your friend has just lost his wallet. Which reaction fits?
questions.explanation: Que chatice expresses sympathy for something annoying. The others celebrate or laugh at the news.

questions.prompt: Which sentence gives advice rather than reporting a fact?
questions.explanation: Devias is the imperfect of dever and softens the sentence into a recommendation.

questions.prompt: Turn the advice into a friendly question: '____ não falas com o senhorio?'
questions.explanation: Porque não plus a present tense verb suggests an action without sounding like an order.

questions.prompt: Reassure a friend, speaking to him as tu: 'Não te ____, ainda há tempo.'
questions.explanation: A negative command to tu uses the subjunctive form, and the pronoun te goes before the verb after não.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'You should talk to the landlord today.'
questions.explanation: Devias carries the advice and the second verb stays in the infinitive.

activities.title: Um problema com o senhorio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend complains about a problem at home and you react, then give advice in three different ways.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estou farto: o senhorio ainda não arranjou o aquecimento.
lines.en: I am fed up: the landlord still has not fixed the heating.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Que chatice! Já lhe telefonaste?
lines.en: What a nuisance! Have you phoned him yet?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Telefonei duas vezes, mas ele nunca atende.
lines.en: I phoned twice, but he never picks up.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então devias mandar-lhe uma mensagem escrita, para ficar registado.
lines.en: Then you should send him a written message, so there is a record.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se ele continuar a ignorar-me?
lines.en: And if he goes on ignoring me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não te preocupes. O melhor é falar com a administração do prédio.
lines.en: Do not worry. The best thing is to talk to the building management.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tens razão. Vou tratar disso amanhã.
lines.en: You are right. I will deal with it tomorrow.

days.title: A tecnologia no dia a dia
days.objective: Talk about phones, laptops and everyday tech problems using telemóvel, ecrã, carregador, palavra-passe and the verbs guardar, apagar, descarregar and ligar.
days.paretoFocus: The tech words in Portugal differ sharply from the ones learners meet first, and asking for a charger or the Wi-Fi password comes up almost daily.
drills: Write five sentences about your phone, each using a different tech noun.
Describe a problem with a device and what you did about it, in the past.
Ask three questions you would really need in a café with a flat battery.

items: Name six pieces of device vocabulary used in Portugal, with their articles.
Give the verbs for saving, deleting, downloading and updating.
How do you say that something broke by itself and that you did something by accident?

activities.title: O telemóvel e o portátil
activities.intro: In Portugal a mobile phone is o telemóvel and a laptop is o portátil, and a screen is o ecrã. Most tech actions use ordinary verbs: guardar to save, apagar to delete, descarregar to download and atualizar to update. Note that a device breaking uses a reflexive verb, partir-se, because nobody is blamed for the action.

items.hr: O meu telemóvel está sem bateria, tens um carregador?
items.en: My phone has no battery left, do you have a charger?
items.note: estar sem = to be out of something

items.hr: Não me consigo ligar à rede, a palavra-passe está errada.
items.en: I cannot connect to the network, the password is wrong.
items.note: palavra-passe = password

items.hr: Vou descarregar a aplicação e depois atualizá-la.
items.en: I am going to download the app and then update it.
items.note: atualizá-la: the infinitive drops its r and the pronoun becomes la

items.hr: Guardei o ficheiro no portátil, mas apaguei-o sem querer.
items.en: I saved the file on the laptop, but I deleted it by accident.
items.note: sem querer = by accident, without meaning to

items.hr: O ecrã partiu-se quando o telemóvel caiu ao chão.
items.en: The screen broke when the phone fell on the floor.
items.note: partir-se describes damage with no agent

activities.title: Falar de aparelhos
activities.intro: Practise the European Portuguese tech nouns and the verbs that go with them.

questions.prompt: Which word do speakers in Portugal use for a mobile phone?
questions.explanation: Telemóvel is the standard word in Portugal. Telefonema means a phone call, not the device.

questions.prompt: Which verb means to save a file?
questions.explanation: Guardar is to keep or save. Apagar is the opposite, to delete.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for a screen: 'O ____ do meu portátil está partido.'
questions.explanation: Ecrã is the European Portuguese word for screen and takes the masculine article o.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb for getting an app onto your phone: 'Vou ____ a aplicação esta noite.'
questions.explanation: Descarregar and transferir both mean to download in Portugal.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Yesterday I deleted the file by accident.'
questions.explanation: Sem querer is a fixed phrase and stays at the end of the sentence.

activities.title: O telemóvel avariado
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend notices your damaged phone and you explain what happened using the tech vocabulary.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O que é que aconteceu ao teu telemóvel?
lines.en: What happened to your phone?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Caiu ao chão e o ecrã partiu-se todo.
lines.en: It fell on the floor and the screen broke completely.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E ainda liga?
lines.en: And does it still turn on?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Liga, mas fica sempre sem bateria. Acho que o carregador também não funciona.
lines.en: It does, but it is always running out of battery. I think the charger does not work either.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guardaste as fotografias nalgum lado?
lines.en: Did you save the photos anywhere?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guardei quase todas no portátil, mas apaguei umas sem querer.
lines.en: I saved almost all of them on the laptop, but I deleted a few by accident.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então leva-o já a uma loja de reparações.
lines.en: Then take it to a repair shop right away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Levo amanhã, assim que atualizar as aplicações.
lines.en: I will take it tomorrow, as soon as I update the apps.

days.title: A música, o cinema e as saídas
days.objective: Invite someone out and arrange the details, using apetece-te, sessão, bilhetes esgotados and combinar for the time and place.
days.paretoFocus: Apetecer works backwards compared to English and appears in every spontaneous invitation, so getting apetece-te right unlocks a whole social register.
drills: Write three invitations with apetecer, one for a film, one for a concert and one for a drink.
Write a two line exchange arranging where and when to meet.
Describe a film you saw recently in three sentences, including its genre.

items: Which pronoun does apetecer take, and where does it go in a plain statement?
Say four words connected with tickets and screenings.
How do you propose a meeting time and place in one short question?

activities.title: Apetece-te sair?
activities.intro: Apetecer works like gostar in reverse: the thing is the subject and the person appears as an indirect pronoun, so apetece-me means I feel like. In an affirmative sentence that pronoun follows the verb with a hyphen. Combinar is the verb Portuguese speakers use for arranging a time and place to meet.

items.hr: Apetece-te ir ao cinema hoje à noite?
items.en: Do you feel like going to the cinema tonight?
items.note: apetece-me, apetece-te, apetece-lhe

items.hr: Há uma sessão às nove e meia, mas os bilhetes estão quase esgotados.
items.en: There is a screening at half past nine, but the tickets are almost sold out.
items.note: sessão = screening; esgotado = sold out

items.hr: É um policial com uma banda sonora fantástica.
items.en: It is a crime film with a fantastic soundtrack.
items.note: policial = crime film; banda sonora = soundtrack

items.hr: Combinamos às oito em frente ao teatro?
items.en: Shall we meet at eight in front of the theatre?
items.note: combinar = to arrange to meet

items.hr: Depois do concerto, podemos ir tomar um copo.
items.en: After the concert we can go for a drink.
items.note: tomar um copo = to go for a drink

activities.title: Combinar uma saída
activities.intro: Practise inviting with apetecer and settling the practical details.

questions.prompt: Which word says that the tickets are sold out?
questions.explanation: Esgotado means sold out. Gratuito means free of charge and atrasado means delayed.

questions.prompt: Complete the invitation: '____ ir ao concerto no sábado?'
questions.explanation: The verb stays in the third person because the activity is the subject, and te follows it with a hyphen.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for a cinema showing: 'A última ____ é às onze da noite.'
questions.explanation: Sessão is the word for a screening or showing at a given time.

questions.prompt: Propose a meeting using the nós form of the verb for arranging: '____ às sete no café da praça?'
questions.explanation: Combinamos in the present works as a proposal, like shall we meet.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'There are still tickets for the half past nine screening.'
questions.explanation: Das nove e meia comes from de plus as, giving the time of the screening.

activities.title: Uma saída à noite
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend suggests going out and together you choose the event, the screening and the meeting point.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Apetece-te sair hoje à noite?
lines.en: Do you feel like going out tonight?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Apetece, sim. Podemos ir ao cinema ou a um concerto.
lines.en: Yes, I do. We could go to the cinema or to a concert.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Há um concerto de fado aqui perto, mas os bilhetes são caros.
lines.en: There is a fado concert near here, but the tickets are expensive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então vamos ao cinema. Estreou um policial com uma banda sonora ótima.
lines.en: Then let's go to the cinema. A crime film with a great soundtrack has just come out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A que horas é a sessão?
lines.en: What time is the screening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Há uma às nove e meia. Combinamos às nove em frente ao cinema?
lines.en: There is one at half past nine. Shall we meet at nine in front of the cinema?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Combinado. E depois vamos tomar um copo.
lines.en: Agreed. And afterwards we will go for a drink.

days.title: No cabeleireiro e outros serviços
days.objective: Ask for a service and check the price and the waiting time at the hairdresser, the dry cleaner's and the shoe repair shop.
days.paretoFocus: Service counters run on three questions, what you want, how long it takes and how much it costs, and queria plus pode are the two polite openings that carry all of them.
drills: Write four requests starting with queria, each for a different service.
Write the two questions you always need about time and price.
Role play leaving an item somewhere and asking when it will be ready.

items: Name four service providers and what each one repairs or does.
Why is queria more polite than quero when you ask for something?
Ask how long something takes and how much it costs altogether.

activities.title: Queria cortar o cabelo
activities.intro: In a shop or salon, queria, the imperfect of querer, replaces quero because it sounds noticeably softer. Requests aimed at the other person use pode plus an infinitive, often with a pronoun attached to that infinitive. Two questions close almost every service exchange: how long it takes and how much it comes to.

items.hr: Queria cortar o cabelo, mas só um bocadinho.
items.en: I would like a haircut, but just a little bit.
items.note: um bocadinho = just a little, very common in Portugal

items.hr: Pode aparar-me a franja, por favor?
items.en: Could you trim my fringe, please?
items.note: aparar = to trim; franja = fringe

items.hr: Deixei um casaco na lavandaria para limpeza a seco.
items.en: I left a coat at the laundry for dry cleaning.
items.note: limpeza a seco = dry cleaning

items.hr: O sapateiro disse que os saltos ficam prontos na quinta.
items.en: The cobbler said the heels will be ready on Thursday.
items.note: ficar pronto = to be ready

items.hr: Quanto tempo demora e quanto fica ao todo?
items.en: How long does it take and how much is it altogether?
items.note: quanto fica = how much does it come to

activities.title: Pedir um serviço
activities.intro: Practise polite requests and the questions about time, price and readiness.

questions.prompt: You want only a small trim. Which sentence fits?
options: Queria pintar o cabelo de louro.
Quero lavar o cabelo em casa.
questions.explanation: Queria softens the request and só um bocadinho limits how much is cut.

questions.prompt: Who repairs shoes?
questions.explanation: O sapateiro repairs shoes, o relojoeiro repairs watches and o canalizador is a plumber.

questions.prompt: Complete the dry cleaning expression: 'Este casaco precisa de limpeza a ____.'
questions.explanation: Limpeza a seco is the fixed expression for dry cleaning.

questions.prompt: Ask when the shoes will be finished: 'Quando é que os sapatos ficam ____?'
questions.explanation: Ficar pronto means to be ready, and the adjective agrees with sapatos.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I left a coat at the laundry yesterday.'
questions.explanation: Na comes from em plus a, because lavandaria is feminine.

activities.title: No cabeleireiro
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are in the hairdresser's chair and have to say exactly what you want, then check time and price.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. O que lhe faço hoje?
lines.en: Good afternoon. What shall I do for you today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Queria cortar o cabelo, mas só um bocadinho atrás.
lines.en: I would like a haircut, but just a little at the back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E a franja? Quer que eu apare também?
lines.en: And the fringe? Do you want me to trim it as well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, pode aparar, mas sem encurtar muito.
lines.en: Yes, you can trim it, but without shortening it much.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quer lavar o cabelo antes?
lines.en: Would you like your hair washed first?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quero, sim. Quanto tempo demora ao todo?
lines.en: Yes, I would. How long does it take altogether?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cerca de quarenta minutos. Fica em vinte euros.
lines.en: About forty minutes. It comes to twenty euros.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Está bem. E quanto fica se pintar também?
lines.en: All right. And how much is it if I have it coloured too?

days.title: Objetos perdidos e pequenos problemas
days.objective: Report a lost item and describe it, using perder, esquecer-se de, dar pela falta de and the vocabulary of perdidos e achados.
days.paretoFocus: Losing something abroad is stressful and needs precise verbs, because esquecer-se de and perder are not interchangeable and the wrong one confuses the person helping you.
drills: Write three sentences about losing different things, using a different verb in each one.
Describe one object of yours in detail: colour, material and what is inside it.
Write the exact question you would ask at a lost property counter.

items: What is the difference between perdi and esqueci-me de?
How do you say that you only noticed something was missing later?
Give four adjectives you would use to describe a bag or a wallet.

activities.title: Perdi a carteira
activities.intro: Perder is used when you do not know where the thing went, while esquecer-se de says you left it somewhere specific. To say you noticed something was missing, Portuguese uses dar pela falta de. Descriptions of the object come after the noun: colour first, then material.

items.hr: Perdi a carteira algures entre o comboio e a estação.
items.en: I lost my wallet somewhere between the train and the station.
items.note: algures = somewhere

items.hr: Esqueci-me do guarda-chuva no autocarro.
items.en: I left my umbrella on the bus.
items.note: esquecer-se de: the thing forgotten takes de

items.hr: Onde fica a secção de perdidos e achados?
items.en: Where is the lost property office?
items.note: perdidos e achados = lost property

items.hr: Só dei pela falta do telemóvel quando cheguei a casa.
items.en: I only noticed the phone was missing when I got home.
items.note: dar pela falta de = to notice something is missing

items.hr: Deixei o meu número, para o caso de alguém a encontrar.
items.en: I left my number, in case somebody finds it.
items.note: para o caso de + infinitive = in case

activities.title: Comunicar uma perda
activities.intro: Practise the verbs of losing and forgetting and the words you need at a lost property counter.

questions.prompt: Which sentence says that you left your umbrella behind on the bus?
questions.explanation: Esquecer-se de names both the object and the place where it stayed. Perder-se means to get lost yourself.

questions.prompt: Where do you go in a station to ask about an item you lost?
questions.explanation: Perdidos e achados is the lost property desk. A bilheteira sells tickets.

questions.prompt: Put perder in the past, eu form: 'Ontem ____ as chaves de casa.'
questions.explanation: Perder is a regular -er verb in the pretérito perfeito: perdi, perdeste, perdeu.

questions.prompt: Complete the expression for noticing an absence: 'Só dei pela ____ do telemóvel à noite.'
questions.explanation: Dar pela falta de is the fixed way of saying you realised something was gone.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Did anybody hand in a black umbrella today?'
questions.explanation: The colour follows the noun in Portuguese, so preto comes after guarda-chuva.

activities.title: Nos perdidos e achados
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You report a lost wallet at a station counter and describe it precisely.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde, em que posso ajudar?
lines.en: Good afternoon, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Perdi a minha carteira esta manhã no comboio.
lines.en: Good afternoon. I lost my wallet this morning on the train.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lembra-se de qual era a carruagem?
lines.en: Do you remember which carriage it was?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não me lembro. Só dei pela falta quando cheguei ao emprego.
lines.en: I do not remember. I only noticed it was missing when I got to work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E como é a carteira?
lines.en: And what does the wallet look like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É castanha, de pele, com um fecho dourado e os meus documentos lá dentro.
lines.en: It is brown, leather, with a golden clasp and my documents inside.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vamos ver amanhã. Se aparecer, telefonamos-lhe.
lines.en: We will check tomorrow. If it turns up, we will phone you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muito obrigado. Deixo aqui o meu número, se faz favor.
lines.en: Thank you very much. I will leave my number here, please.

days.title: Reciclar e os gestos ecológicos simples
days.objective: Sort waste and talk about simple green habits using vidrão, papelão, ecoponto, separar o lixo and the patterns basta and é preciso plus an infinitive.
days.paretoFocus: The Portuguese recycling bins have their own names that no dictionary habit will teach you, and basta plus an infinitive is a compact way to give general advice.
drills: Sort ten items from your kitchen bin into the right Portuguese container, in writing.
Write three general recommendations with basta or é preciso plus an infinitive.
Describe two green habits you already have and one you would like to start.

items: Which container takes glass, which takes paper and which colour takes plastic packaging?
How do basta and é preciso differ in strength when giving advice?
Say three simple ways to save water or energy at home.

activities.title: Separar o lixo
activities.intro: Portugal names its recycling containers by material: o vidrão for glass, o papelão for paper and card, and the yellow ecoponto for plastic and metal packaging. General recommendations avoid naming a person, using é preciso plus an infinitive for a requirement and basta plus an infinitive for something easy and sufficient.

items.hr: Em Portugal, o vidro vai para o vidrão e o papel para o papelão.
items.en: In Portugal, glass goes in the glass bank and paper in the paper bank.
items.note: vidrão and papelão are the street containers

items.hr: As embalagens de plástico deitam-se no ecoponto amarelo.
items.en: Plastic packaging is thrown into the yellow container.
items.note: deitar fora = to throw away

items.hr: Basta apagar as luzes ao sair para poupar energia.
items.en: Just turning the lights off when you leave is enough to save energy.
items.note: basta + infinitive = it is enough to

items.hr: É preciso separar o lixo antes de o deitar fora.
items.en: You have to sort the rubbish before throwing it away.
items.note: antes de o deitar: the pronoun sits before the infinitive here

items.hr: Levo sempre uma garrafa reutilizável e sacos de pano.
items.en: I always carry a reusable bottle and cloth bags.
items.note: sacos de pano = cloth bags

activities.title: Gestos do dia a dia
activities.intro: Practise the names of the containers and the impersonal advice patterns.

questions.prompt: Where do glass bottles go in Portugal?
questions.explanation: O vidrão takes glass. O pilhão is only for used batteries.

questions.prompt: Which container takes plastic and metal packaging?
questions.explanation: Yellow is for packaging, blue for paper and green for glass.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb for sorting rubbish: 'É preciso ____ o lixo em casa.'
questions.explanation: Separar o lixo is the standard expression for sorting waste.

questions.prompt: Use the verb that means it is enough to: 'Para poupar energia, ____ apagar as luzes ao sair.'
questions.explanation: Basta stays in the third person singular and is followed directly by an infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Turning the lights off saves a lot of energy.'
questions.explanation: An infinitive can act as the subject of the sentence, so the verb poupa stays singular.

activities.title: Separar o lixo em casa
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A flatmate does not know the system yet and you explain where everything goes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onde é que ponho estas garrafas de vidro?
lines.en: Where do I put these glass bottles?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No vidrão, lá em baixo, ao lado do papelão.
lines.en: In the glass bank downstairs, next to the paper bank.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as embalagens de plástico?
lines.en: And the plastic packaging?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Essas vão para o ecoponto amarelo. É preciso separar tudo.
lines.en: Those go in the yellow container. Everything has to be sorted.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dá algum trabalho, não dá?
lines.en: It is a bit of work, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ao início, sim, mas depois torna-se um hábito.
lines.en: At first, yes, but then it becomes a habit.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o que mais fazes para poupar?
lines.en: And what else do you do to save resources?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Basta apagar as luzes ao sair e levar sacos de pano às compras.
lines.en: It is enough to turn off the lights when leaving and take cloth bags shopping.

days.title: Comparar duas cidades, dois produtos, dois hábitos
days.objective: Build a balanced comparison using irregular comparatives such as maior and melhor, the equality pattern tão como, and the contrast markers por um lado, por outro and enquanto.
days.paretoFocus: Anyone can say mais caro, but the irregular comparatives and the contrast markers are what let you weigh two options instead of just labelling one.
drills: Compare two cities you know in four sentences, using a different structure each time.
Write one sentence with tão como and one with the irregular form of bem.
Contrast your own habit with someone else's using enquanto.

items: Give the irregular comparatives of grande, pequeno, bom and bem.
How do you say that two things are equally expensive?
Which two markers let you present an advantage and then a drawback?

activities.title: Por um lado, por outro
activities.intro: Four adjectives have their own comparative form and never take mais: grande gives maior, pequeno gives menor, bom gives melhor and bem gives melhor as well. To say two things are equal, use tão plus the adjective plus como. To weigh two sides of the same question, use por um lado and por outro, and to contrast two habits, use enquanto.

items.hr: Lisboa é maior do que o Porto, mas o Porto é mais acolhedor.
items.en: Lisbon is bigger than Porto, but Porto is more welcoming.
items.note: maior already means bigger, so mais maior is wrong

items.hr: Este telemóvel é tão caro como o outro, mas dura mais.
items.en: This phone is as expensive as the other one, but it lasts longer.
items.note: tão + adjective + como = as ... as

items.hr: Por um lado, o campo é calmo; por outro, fica longe de tudo.
items.en: On the one hand the countryside is quiet, on the other it is far from everything.
items.note: por um lado ... por outro weighs two sides

items.hr: Eu ando sempre a pé, enquanto o meu irmão vai de carro.
items.en: I always walk, whereas my brother goes by car.
items.note: enquanto contrasts two habits at the same time

items.hr: A diferença é que aqui os transportes funcionam melhor.
items.en: The difference is that here public transport works better.
items.note: melhor is the comparative of bem and never takes mais

activities.title: Pesar duas opções
activities.intro: Practise the irregular comparatives, the equality pattern and the contrast markers.

questions.prompt: Which comparative sentence is correct?
options: O Porto é mais grande que Braga.
O Porto é maior do que Braga.
O Porto é mais maior que Braga.
O Porto é o mais grande de Braga.
questions.answer: O Porto é maior do que Braga.
questions.explanation: Grande has the irregular comparative maior, which cannot be combined with mais.

questions.prompt: Complete the equality: 'Este portátil é ____ caro como o outro.'
questions.explanation: Before an adjective the form is tão. Tanto is used with nouns and verbs.

questions.prompt: Use the comparative of bem: 'Aqui os transportes funcionam ____ do que na minha terra.'
questions.explanation: Bem becomes melhor in comparisons, never mais bem in this sense.

questions.prompt: Contrast two habits with one word: 'Eu ando a pé, ____ o meu irmão vai sempre de carro.'
questions.explanation: Enquanto sets two simultaneous habits against each other.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'This phone is cheaper, but it lasts less time.'
questions.explanation: Barato is regular, so it takes mais, while menos tempo gives the drawback.

activities.title: Lisboa ou Porto?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks you to compare two cities and you weigh both sides instead of choosing at once.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Preferes viver em Lisboa ou no Porto?
lines.en: Would you rather live in Lisbon or in Porto?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lisboa é maior e tem mais trabalho, mas o Porto é mais acolhedor.
lines.en: Lisbon is bigger and has more work, but Porto is more welcoming.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as rendas? São iguais?
lines.en: And the rents? Are they the same?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não. No Porto as rendas são mais baixas, enquanto em Lisboa quase tudo é caro.
lines.en: No. In Porto the rents are lower, whereas in Lisbon almost everything is expensive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E os transportes, como são?
lines.en: And what about public transport?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Por um lado, em Lisboa há mais metro; por outro, no Porto ando quase sempre a pé.
lines.en: On the one hand Lisbon has more metro, on the other in Porto I walk nearly everywhere.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então qual é a grande diferença?
lines.en: So what is the big difference?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A diferença é que o Porto é tão bonito como Lisboa, mas muito mais calmo.
lines.en: The difference is that Porto is as beautiful as Lisbon, but far quieter.

days.title: Rever o essencial: os erros mais frequentes
days.objective: Spot and correct the five mistakes learners of European Portuguese repeat most: pronoun position after não, saber against conhecer, há for time, por against para, and muito against muitos.
days.paretoFocus: These five errors appear in almost every learner sentence, so fixing them raises how correct you sound far more than adding new vocabulary would.
drills: Write five wrong sentences on purpose, one per error, then write the corrected version beside each.
Say five sentences about your life using há with a period of time.
Write three sentences that contain both por and para correctly.

items: Where does the pronoun go once the sentence starts with não?
When do you use saber and when do you use conhecer?
Which form is invariable, muito or muitos, and in which position?

activities.title: Os erros mais comuns
activities.intro: Five habits fix most learner errors at this level. After não, and after words like nunca and ninguém, the pronoun moves in front of the verb. Saber covers facts and skills while conhecer covers people and places. Há plus a length of time replaces the English for, por gives the reason and para the destination, and muito only agrees when it sits before a noun.

items.hr: Não me lembro do nome dele.
items.en: I do not remember his name.
items.note: after não the pronoun goes before the verb, never lembro-me

items.hr: Sei conduzir, mas não conheço bem esta cidade.
items.en: I can drive, but I do not know this city well.
items.note: saber for skills and facts, conhecer for places and people

items.hr: Estou em Lisboa há dois anos.
items.en: I have been in Lisbon for two years.
items.note: há plus a period of time, written with h

items.hr: Este presente é para ti, obrigado por teres vindo.
items.en: This present is for you, thank you for coming.
items.note: para marks the recipient, por marks the reason

items.hr: Gosto muito de peixe e como muitos legumes.
items.en: I like fish a lot and I eat a lot of vegetables.
items.note: muito is invariable with a verb, muitos agrees with the noun

activities.title: Corrigir os erros
activities.intro: Practise choosing the correct form in the five situations that trip learners up most.

questions.prompt: Which negative sentence places the pronoun correctly?
questions.explanation: Não pulls the pronoun in front of the verb, so the hyphenated form disappears.

questions.prompt: Complete both gaps: 'Eu ____ conduzir, mas não ____ esta zona.'
questions.explanation: Saber is used for a skill such as driving, conhecer for familiarity with a place.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word that measures a length of time: 'Vivo em Lisboa ____ dois anos.'
questions.explanation: Há plus a time span translates the English for, and it is written with an h.

questions.prompt: Choose the right form before a noun: 'Como ____ legumes todos os dias.'
questions.explanation: Before a plural masculine noun the quantifier agrees, giving muitos.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I have been living in Porto for three years.'
questions.explanation: The verb stays in the present because the situation is still true, and há introduces the duration.

activities.title: Ainda troco por e para
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A Portuguese friend asks about your progress and you talk about the mistakes you still make, using the corrected forms.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vives em Portugal desde quando?
lines.en: Since when have you been living in Portugal?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vivo cá há dois anos, mas ainda não conheço bem o país.
lines.en: I have lived here for two years, but I still do not know the country well.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E já sabes falar com os vizinhos sem problemas?
lines.en: And can you already talk to the neighbours without any trouble?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sei, mas às vezes ainda troco por e para.
lines.en: I can, but sometimes I still mix up por and para.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso é normal. Lembras-te da regra?
lines.en: That is normal. Do you remember the rule?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não me lembro sempre. Por é a razão, para é o destino.
lines.en: I do not always remember. Por is the reason, para is the destination.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exatamente. E dizes obrigado ou obrigada?
lines.en: Exactly. And do you say obrigado or obrigada?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Depende de quem fala, não de quem ouve.
lines.en: It depends on who is speaking, not on who is listening.

days.title: Revisão A2 III
days.objective: Consolidate the whole block: chores, repairs, housing, comparisons, sport, beach and festivals.
days.paretoFocus: Reactivating this block's six themes in one session cements them far better than re-studying each day separately.
drills: In two minutes, say one sentence per theme: lida da casa, avaria, casa nova, comparação, desporto, festa.
Compare your ideal flat with your current one using mais/menos/tão... como.
Tell the story of a perfect Portuguese Saturday: cleaning, gym, beach, festa.

items: Recall five chore verbs without looking.
Say the irregular comparatives and superlatives: melhor, pior, maior, o melhor.
Translate: 'The landlord fixed the water heater before the move.'

activities.title: Tudo num só dia
activities.intro: One key sentence per theme of the block, read, understand, and say each one aloud.

items.hr: Antes de sair, aspirei a casa e estendi a roupa.
items.en: Before going out, I vacuumed the house and hung out the laundry.
items.note: chores in the past, perfeito simples

items.hr: O esquentador avariou, mas o técnico arranjou-o no mesmo dia.
items.en: The water heater broke down, but the technician fixed it the same day.
items.note: avariar + arranjar, the repair pair

items.hr: O senhorio baixou a renda porque o T2 não tem elevador.
items.en: The landlord lowered the rent because the flat has no lift.
items.note: housing vocabulary in context

items.hr: Este bairro é mais barato do que o centro, mas é tão bonito como.
items.en: This neighbourhood is cheaper than the centre, but it's just as pretty.
items.note: comparatives combined in one sentence

items.hr: Depois do ginásio, a equipa foi comer gelados à beira-mar.
items.en: After the gym, the team went to eat ice creams by the sea.
items.note: sport + beach vocabulary together

items.hr: Convidei os vizinhos para comer sardinhas assadas na festa do prédio.
items.en: I invited the neighbours to eat grilled sardines at the building party.
items.note: convidar para + festival food

activities.title: Miscelânea do bloco
activities.intro: Mixed questions from all the themes of this block. No hints, trust your memory.

questions.prompt: Which task needs 'o aspirador'?
questions.explanation: The vacuum cleaner ('o aspirador') is used to vacuum ('aspirar').

questions.prompt: The tap has been dripping for a week. Which sentence reports it correctly?
options: A torneira está a pingar há uma semana.
questions.answer: A torneira está a pingar há uma semana.
questions.explanation: 'Estar a + infinitive' + 'há + duration' expresses an ongoing situation in pt-PT.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct comparison: 'O Porto é ___ antigo ___ Lisboa? Discutam!'
questions.explanation: Equality uses 'tão + adjective + como'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the housing word: 'Paguei a ___ ao senhorio no dia um do mês.'
questions.explanation: You pay 'a renda' (the rent) to the landlord each month.

questions.prompt: Complete with the pt-PT football word: 'A equipa festejou quando o capitão marcou o ___ da vitória.'
questions.explanation: In Portugal a goal in football is 'um golo'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the -íssimo form of 'caro': 'Alugar casa no centro está ___ este ano.'
questions.explanation: Caro → caríssimo, the absolute superlative: extremely expensive.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'After the beach we went to the neighbourhood party.'
questions.explanation: Time phrase + perfeito simples of 'ir' (fomos) + destination with 'à'.

activities.title: Pôr a conversa em dia
activities.intro: You bump into a friend and catch up on everything from the block. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Olá! Há quanto tempo! Então, já encontraste casa?
lines.en: Hi! Long time no see! So, have you found a place yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Encontrei! Um T2 perto da praia, mais barato do que o meu antigo apartamento.
lines.en: I did! A two-bedroom near the beach, cheaper than my old flat.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que sorte! E a mudança, correu bem?
lines.en: Lucky you! And the move, did it go well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mais ou menos... a máquina de lavar avariou logo na primeira semana.
lines.en: More or less... the washing machine broke down in the very first week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah não! Chamaste um técnico?
lines.en: Oh no! Did you call a technician?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Chamei, e já está arranjada. Agora vem ao jantar de sábado, faço anos e vai haver sardinhas!
lines.en: I did, and it's fixed now. Now come to Saturday's dinner, it's my birthday and there'll be sardines!

days.title: Ponto de controlo A2 (CIPLE)
days.objective: Test yourself against CIPLE-style tasks: reading short authentic texts and handling a services situation, the skills of the A2 certificate exam.
days.paretoFocus: CIPLE reading is mostly notices, signs and short messages: learn to scan for who, when, where and how much, and half the exam is yours.
drills: Read a shop notice aloud and answer in one sentence: what changed and from when?
Roleplay buying stamps and sending a parcel at the post office, out loud.
Summarise a short text message in one sentence: who wrote it and what do they want?

items: Recall the CIPLE format: reading/writing, listening, and a short spoken interaction.
Say three words you expect on Portuguese notices: encerrado, horário, avariado.
Translate: 'The pharmacy is closed on Sundays.'

activities.title: Ler avisos e mensagens
activities.intro: The short authentic texts that appear in the CIPLE exam: notices, signs and quick messages.

items.hr: AVISO: O elevador está avariado. Pedimos desculpa pelo incómodo.
items.en: NOTICE: The lift is out of order. We apologise for the inconvenience.
items.note: typical building notice, 'pedimos desculpa pelo incómodo' is a fixed formula

items.hr: Encerrado para férias de 1 a 15 de agosto. Reabrimos dia 16.
items.en: Closed for holidays from the 1st to the 15th of August. We reopen on the 16th.
items.note: 'encerrado' = closed (formal, on signs); reabrir = to reopen

items.hr: Horário de atendimento: das 9h às 13h e das 14h às 18h.
items.en: Opening hours: from 9 to 1 and from 2 to 6.
items.note: 'horário de atendimento' appears on almost every service door

items.hr: Promoção: leve três, pague dois. Válida até domingo.
items.en: Special offer: take three, pay for two. Valid until Sunday.
items.note: supermarket promotion language, 'válida até' = valid until

items.hr: Olá Marta! O jantar mudou para as 20h30. Podes trazer a sobremesa? Beijinhos.
items.en: Hi Marta! Dinner has moved to 8:30 pm. Can you bring dessert? Kisses.
items.note: a short personal message, CIPLE loves these

items.hr: Farmácia de serviço: aberta 24 horas. Em caso de urgência, ligue 112.
items.en: Duty pharmacy: open 24 hours. In case of emergency, call 112.
items.note: 'farmácia de serviço' = the on-duty pharmacy; 112 = emergency number

activities.title: Compreensão tipo CIPLE
activities.intro: Read each short text and answer as in the exam: one detail decides the correct option.

questions.prompt: Sign on a door: 'Encerrado para obras. Reabrimos em setembro.' What does it mean?
options: The shop is closed for renovation until September.
The shop is open only in September.
The shop closed forever in September.
The shop opens earlier in September.
questions.answer: The shop is closed for renovation until September.
questions.explanation: 'Encerrado para obras' = closed for works; 'reabrimos em setembro' = we reopen in September.

questions.prompt: Notice: 'Consultas só com marcação. Ligue 218 000 000.' What must you do to get an appointment?
questions.explanation: 'Só com marcação' = by appointment only, and the notice gives a number to call.

questions.prompt: Message: 'Pai, cheguei bem a Coimbra. O comboio atrasou-se uma hora. Ligo à noite.' What happened?
options: The train was an hour late.
questions.answer: The train was an hour late.
questions.explanation: 'O comboio atrasou-se uma hora' = the train was delayed by one hour.

questions.prompt: Supermarket flyer: 'Promoção válida até sábado, apenas em loja.' When does the offer end?
questions.explanation: 'Válida até sábado' = valid until Saturday, after that the offer ends.

questions.prompt: Complete the notice with the formal word for closed: 'O museu está ___ à segunda-feira.'
questions.explanation: On Portuguese signs, closed is 'encerrado'; museums typically close on Mondays.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Em caso de urgência à noite, procure a farmácia de ___.'
questions.explanation: The 'farmácia de serviço' is the duty pharmacy open outside normal hours.

questions.prompt: Complete the post-office request: 'Queria enviar esta ___ para França, por correio registado.'
questions.explanation: 'Uma encomenda' = a parcel; 'correio registado' = registered mail.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The pharmacy is open until eight in the evening.'
questions.explanation: 'Até às + time' expresses closing time, a key pattern for reading opening hours.

activities.title: Nos correios
activities.intro: A CIPLE-style services situation: you need to send a parcel at the post office. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia, é a sua vez. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.en: Good morning, it's your turn. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Queria enviar esta encomenda para Espanha, por favor.
lines.en: Good morning. I'd like to send this parcel to Spain, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com certeza. Correio normal ou registado?
lines.en: Certainly. Normal or registered mail?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Registado, porque é uma prenda importante. Quanto custa?
lines.en: Registered, because it's an important present. How much does it cost?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: São nove euros e vinte. Chega em três dias úteis. Precisa de mais alguma coisa?
lines.en: That's nine euros twenty. It arrives in three working days. Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, queria também dois selos para postais. Obrigado pela ajuda!
lines.en: Yes, I'd also like two stamps for postcards. Thanks for your help!

resources: CAPLE, Exames de PLE (Universidade de Lisboa)
